# MuleSoft MCP Servers - Full Documentation

# Getting Started with MuleSoft Platform MCP Server

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Scan, discover, govern, and monitor APIs, agents, MCP servers, and LLMs across your entire IT
landscape from a single AI-accessible interface. MuleSoft Platform MCP Server gives AI tools
governed access to your infrastructure to accelerate production-ready AI deployment. Connect your
IDE in minutes using any MCP-compatible client.

## Create a Connected App

Follow the steps in
[Create a Connected App That Acts on the Userâs Behalf](../access-management/creating-connected-apps-dev#create-connected-app)
to create a connected app.

Additionally, when prompted, add the following permission scopes. These scopes enable you to use
specific MuleSoft Platform MCP Server tools.

> [!IMPORTANT] When creating the connected app, be sure to select the Authorization Code and Refresh
> Token grant types.

Refer to the [MuleSoft Platform MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools-platform) for details
on all MuleSoft Platform MCP Server tools. Only tools listed in these tables require specific
permission scopes.

### Exchange

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Associated Tools</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View and download assets within a business ## Configure Your IDE

Configure Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Cursor, Windsurf, or VS Code to work with MuleSoft Platform MCP
Server. After you add the configuration, verify it by checking that the server has started in the
MCP section of your IDE.

> [!IMPORTANT] MuleSoft remote MCP servers currently only support manual client registration through
> app creation and do not support other client registration methods such as Dynamic Client
> Registration (DCR).

### Available Client Connections

When configuring your AI tool, use the endpoint for your region.

Use `/mcp`, and fall back to `/mcp/sse` if the client does not connect.

- [https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp](https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp)
- [https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp/sse](https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp/sse)

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>URL</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>US Prod</p></td><td><p><a href="https://omni.mulesoft.com">omni.mulesoft.com</a> (alias) / <a href="https://us1.omni.mulesoft.com">us1.omni.mulesoft.com</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>EU Prod</p></td><td><p><a href="https://eu1.omni.mulesoft.com">eu1.omni.mulesoft.com</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CA Prod</p></td><td><p><a href="https://ca1.omni.mulesoft.com">ca1.omni.mulesoft.com</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>JP Prod</p></td><td><p><a href="https://jp1.omni.mulesoft.com">jp1.omni.mulesoft.com</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>IN Prod</p></td><td><p><a href="https://in1.omni.mulesoft.com">in1.omni.mulesoft.com</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

For more information about Anypoint Platform regions, see
[Control Plane Hosting Options](../hosting-home/#control-plane-hosting-options).

### Claude Desktop

In Claude Desktop:

1.  Click **Customize** > **Connectors**.
2.  Click the add (+) icon, and then click **Add Custom Connector**.
3.  Name the connection, and enter the **Remote Universal URL**, for example,
    [https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp](https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp).
4.  Enter the Client ID and Client Secret from your connected app.

In your connected app:

1.  Log into Anypoint Platform, and open your connected app.
2.  In the **Redirect URLs** field, enter
    `[https://claude.ai/api/mcp/auth_callback](https://claude.ai/api/mcp/auth_callback)`.
3.  To apply changes, click **Continue**.

OR run this command in your terminal:

```bash
MCP_CLIENT_SECRET=<YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET> claude mcp add --transport http mulesoft-platform YOUR_URL_HERE --client-id <YOUR_CLIENT_ID>
```

Download [Claude Desktop](https://claude.com/download).

### ChatGPT

In ChatGPT:

1.  Click **Apps** > **Settings.**
2.  Click the settings icon, click **Create App**, and configure the following:
    1.  Name the connection, and enter the **Remote Universal URL**, for example,
        [https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp](https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp).

3.  In **OAuth** > **Advanced settings** > **Client registration**, configure the following:
    1.  Enter the Client ID and Client Secret from your connected app.
    2.  Copy the Callback URL.

In your connected app:

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform, and open your connected app.
2.  In the redirect URLs field, enter the Callback URL value you copied.
3.  To apply changes, click **Continue**.

Download [ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/download/).

### Cursor

In Cursor:

1.  Click **Settings** > **Tools and MCPs** > **Add a Custom MCP Server**.
2.  Add the following code snippet:

    ```json
    {
      "mcpServers": {
        "mulesoft-platform": {
          "command": "npx",
          "args": [
            "-y",
            "mcp-remote@latest",
            "https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp",
            "--static-oauth-client-info",
            "{\"client_id\":\"YOUR_CLIENT_ID\",\"client_secret\":\"YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET HERE\"}"
          ]
        }
      }
    }
    ```

Download [Cursor](https://cursor.com/).

### Windsurf

In Windsurf:

1.  Click **Settings** > **Cascade** > **Open MCP Registry**.
2.  Click the settings icon and in `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json` add the following code
    snippet:

    ```json
    {
      "mcpServers": {
        "mulesoft-platform": {
          "command": "npx",
          "args": [
            "-y",
            "mcp-remote@latest",
            "https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp",
            "--static-oauth-client-info",
            "{\"client_id\":\"YOUR_CLIENT_ID\",\"client_secret\":\"YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET\"}"
          ]
        }
      }
    }
    ```

Download [Windsurf](https://windsurf.com/).

### VS Code

In VS Code:

1.  In your user profile, configure a custom MCP server in the `mcp.json` file. Alternatively, run
    `MCP: Add Server` in the Command Palette (Shift+Cmd+P) to add a server through a guided flow.

    ```json
    {
      "servers": {
        "mulesoft-platform": {
          "type": "stdio",
          "command": "npx",
          "args": [
            "-y",
            "mcp-remote@latest",
            "https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp",
            "--static-oauth-client-info",
            "{\"client_id\":\"YOUR_CLIENT_ID\",\"client_secret\":\"YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET\"}"
          ]
        }
      }
    }
    ```

2.  To run the server, click **Start**.
3.  When prompted, enter the Client ID and Client Secret from your connected app.

Download [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/).

## Troubleshooting

Resolve authentication and connection issues with these troubleshooting steps.

### Authentication Failure

To resolve authentication issues, follow these troubleshooting steps.

- Try logging out of your Anypoint Platform account and logging back in.
- If you encounter an authorization error:
  - Add the correct redirect URL to your connected app.
  - Add the necessary permissions scopes to your connected app.

- If you get an error when adding a non-traditional redirect URL to your connected app:
  - Determine the callback port automatically assigned for the clientâs authentication request (for
    example, 12148).
  - Add the localhost URL to the redirect URLs in your connected app (for example,
    `[http://localhost:12148/oauth/callback](http://localhost:12148/oauth/callback)`).

- If you get a permissions-related error when attempting to authenticate, add these permissions to
  your connected app:
  - Background Access
  - Profile
  - Identity
  - Email

### Connection Issues

If your MCP client canât reach the server or fails to establish a session, verify these settings.

- Ensure your MCP client supports Streamable HTTP transport.
- Check that your client can handle OAuth authentication flows.
- Verify the MCP server URL is [https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp](https://omni.mulesoft.com/mcp).

## See Also

- [Using MuleSoft Platform MCP Server](using-mulesoft-platform-mcp-server)
---

# Getting Started with MuleSoft DX MCP Server

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft DX MCP Server enables AI-powered development assistants in your IDE to interact with
Anypoint Platform, helping you manage APIs, applications, and platform resources directly from your
coding environment. Install prerequisites, create a connected app with the scopes your tools
require, and register the server in the IDE so assistants can call Anypoint Platform with your
environments and identities.

> [!IMPORTANT] If you have Anypoint Code Builder or Connector Builder, MuleSoft DX MCP Server is
> already installed and configured in MuleSoft Vibes. For more information, see
> [Creating API Specs with MuleSoft Vibes](../anypoint-code-builder/vibes-api-ai-create-spec) and
> [Connector Builder Overview](../connector-builder/).

## Before You Begin

Before you install and use MuleSoft DX MCP Server, ensure you have installed the following software:

- Anypoint Extension Pack version 1.10.0 or later
- Git
- Node.js 20 or later. Download [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/en/download).

You also need permissions or access to perform these tasks:

- Organization administrator access:
  [create a connected app](../access-management/connected-apps-overview)
- [Agentforce](../access-management/enabling-agentforce) access: run the `generate_mule_flow` and
  `generate_api_spec` tools.

## Create a Connected App

Follow the steps in
[Create a Connected App That Acts on Its Own Behalf](../access-management/creating-connected-apps-dev#create-connected-app-on-its-own-behalf)
to create a connected app. Note the Client ID and Client Secret, as you need these to configure
MuleSoft DX MCP Server in your IDE.

Additionally, when prompted, add the following permission scopes. These scopes enable you to use
specific MuleSoft DX MCP Server tools.

Refer to the [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools) for details on all
MuleSoft DX MCP Server tools. Only tools listed in these tables require specific permission scopes.

### Anypoint Code Builder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Associated Tool</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Use natural language prompts to develop and generate flows using Agentforce for Anypoint Code Builder Generative Flows.</p></td><td><p>Mule Developer Generative AI User</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#generate-mule-flow">generate_mule_flow</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#generate-api-spec">generate_api_spec</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#create-mcp-server">create_MCP_server</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#generate-munit-test">generate_munit_test</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#modify-munit-test">modify_munit_test</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Anypoint Monitoring

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Associated Tool</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View but not modify content in Anypoint Monitoring.</p></td><td><p>Monitoring Viewer</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-platform-insights">get_platform_insights</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-reuse-metrics">get_reuse_metrics</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#list-applications">list_applications</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### API Manager

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Associated Tool</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View and modify API configurations in the specified environment.</p></td><td><p>Manage API Configuration</p></td><td><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#create-and-manage-api-instances">create_and_manage_api_instances</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View, create, modify, and delete API policies in the specified environment.</p></td><td><p>Manage Policies</p></td><td><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#manage-api-instance-policy">manage_api_instance_policy</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View API configurations in the specified environment.</p></td><td><p>View APIs Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#list-api-instances">list_api_instances</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-platform-insights">get_platform_insights</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-reuse-metrics">get_reuse_metrics</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Exchange

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Associated Tool</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View, create, and download assets within a business group.</p></li><li><p>Edit asset portal content in an existing asset version.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Exchange Administrator</p></td><td><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#create-and-manage-assets">create_and_manage_assets</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View, create, and download assets within a business group.</p></td><td><p>Exchange Contributor</p></td><td><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#create-and-manage-assets">create_and_manage_assets</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create new assets within a business groupâs catalog.</p></td><td><p>Exchange Creator</p></td><td><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#create-and-manage-assets">create_and_manage_assets</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download assets within a business group.</p></td><td><p>Exchange Viewer</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#search-asset">search_asset</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#deploy-mule-application">deploy_mule_application</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#update-mule-application">update_mule_application</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#implement-api-spec">implement_api_spec</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### General

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Associated Tool</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View organization information.</p></td><td><p>View Organization</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-platform-insights">get_platform_insights</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#update-mule-application">update_mule_application</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View connected applications.</p></td><td><p>View Connected Applications</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-reuse-metrics">get_reuse_metrics</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#list-applications">list_applications</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#deploy-mule-application">deploy_mule_application</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#update-mule-application">update_mule_application</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Runtime Manager

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Associated Tool</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View applications in a specific environment.</p></td><td><p>Read Applications</p></td><td><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#list-applications">list_applications</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create applications in a specific environment.</p></td><td><p>Create Applications</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#deploy-mule-application">deploy_mule_application</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#update-mule-application">update_mule_application</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Runtime Fabric instances in the organization.</p></td><td><p>Read Runtime Fabrics</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-platform-insights">get_platform_insights</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-reuse-metrics">get_reuse_metrics</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#list-applications">list_applications</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#deploy-mule-application">deploy_mule_application</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View CloudHub and CloudHub 2.0 network resources.</p></td><td><p>CloudHub Network Viewer</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-platform-insights">get_platform_insights</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-reuse-metrics">get_reuse_metrics</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Usage

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Associated Tool</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View usage reports.</p></td><td><p>Usage Viewer</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-platform-insights">get_platform_insights</a></p></li><li><p><a href="reference-mcp-tools#get-reuse-metrics">get_reuse_metrics</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Install MuleSoft DX MCP Server

Install MuleSoft DX MCP Server and then configure it in your IDE by adding the appropriate
configuration snippet with your connected app credentials.

To install with Node.js, run: `npm install -g mulesoft-mcp-server`.

### Configure Your IDE

You can configure Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, and other IDEs to work with the MuleSoft DX
MCP Server. After you add the configuration, verify it by checking that the server has started in
the MCP section of your IDE.

For more information about Anypoint Platform regions, see
[Control Plane Hosting Options](../hosting-home/#control-plane-hosting-options).

#### Claude Desktop

Add this snippet to `claude_desktop_config.json` where `ANYPOINT_REGION` (optional) is one of these
values: `PROD_US`, `PROD_EU`, `PROD_CA`, or `PROD_JP`.

{ "mcpServers": { "mulesoft": { "command": "npx", "args": \["-y", "mulesoft-mcp-server", "start"\],
"env": { "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_ID": "<YOUR_CLIENT_ID>", "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_SECRET": "<YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET>",
"ANYPOINT_REGION": "<REGION_NAME>" } } } }

Download [Claude Desktop](https://claude.com/download).

#### Cline

Add this snippet to Cline `config.json` where `ANYPOINT_REGION` (optional) is one of these values:
`PROD_US`, `PROD_EU`, `PROD_CA`, or `PROD_JP`.

{ "mcpServers": { "mulesoft": { "command": "npx", "args": \["-y", "mulesoft-mcp-server", "start"\],
"env": { "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_ID": "<YOUR_CLIENT_ID>", "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_SECRET": "<YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET>",
"ANYPOINT_REGION": "<REGION_NAME>" } } } }

Download [Cline](https://cline.bot/).

#### Cursor

Add this snippet to Cursor `mcp.json` where `ANYPOINT_REGION` (optional) is one of these values:
`PROD_US`, `PROD_EU`, `PROD_CA`, or `PROD_JP`.

{ "mcpServers": { "mulesoft": { "command": "npx", "args": \["-y", "mulesoft-mcp-server", "start"\],
"env": { "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_ID": "<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_ID>", "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_SECRET":
"<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_SECRET>", "ANYPOINT_REGION": "<REGION_NAME>" } } } }

Download [Cursor](https://cursor.com/).

#### Trae

Add this snippet to Trae `mcp_settings.json` or `.vscode/mcp.json` where `ANYPOINT_REGION`
(optional) is one of these values: `PROD_US`, `PROD_EU`, `PROD_CA`, or `PROD_JP`.

{ "mcpServers": { "mulesoft": { "command": "npx", "args": \["-y", "mulesoft-mcp-server", "start"\],
"env": { "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_ID": "<YYOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_ID>", "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_SECRET":
"<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_SECRET>", "ANYPOINT_REGION": "<REGION_NAME>" } } } }

Download [Trae](https://www.trae.ai/).

#### VS Code

Add this snippet to VS Code `settings.json` or `.vscode/mcp.json` where (optional) `ANYPOINT_REGION`
is one of these values: `PROD_US`, `PROD_EU`, `PROD_CA`, or `PROD_JP`.

{ "mcp": { "servers": { "mulesoft": { "command": "npx", "args": \["-y", "mulesoft-mcp-server",
"start"\], "env": { "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_ID": "<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_ID>",
"ANYPOINT_CLIENT_SECRET": "<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_SECRET>", "ANYPOINT_REGION": "<REGION_NAME>" }
} } } }

Download [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/).

#### Windsurf

Add this snippet to Windsurf `mcp_config.json` where `ANYPOINT_REGION` (optional) is one of these
values: `PROD_US`, `PROD_EU`, `PROD_CA`, or `PROD_JP`.

{ "mcpServers": { "mulesoft": { "command": "npx", "args": \["-y", "mulesoft-mcp-server", "start"\],
"env": { "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_ID": "<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_ID>", "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_SECRET":
"<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_SECRET>", "ANYPOINT_REGION": "<REGION_NAME>" } } } }

Download [Windsurf](https://windsurf.com/).

#### Zed

Add this snippet to Zed `settings.json` where `ANYPOINT_REGION` (optional) is one of these values:
`PROD_US`, `PROD_EU`, `PROD_CA`, or `PROD_JP`.

{ "context_servers": { "mulesoft": { "command": "npx", "args": \["-y", "mulesoft-mcp-server",
"start"\], "env": { "ANYPOINT_CLIENT_ID": "<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_ID>",
"ANYPOINT_CLIENT_SECRET": "<YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_SECRET>", "ANYPOINT_REGION": "<REGION_NAME>" }
} } }

Download [Zed](https://github.com/zed-industries/zed).

## See Also

- [Using MuleSoft DX MCP Server](using-mulesoft-mcp-server)
---

# MuleSoft MCP Servers Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft MCP Servers implement the Model Context Protocol (MCP) so AI assistants in your IDE can
work with MuleSoft products and services. MuleSoft DX MCP Server supports API design, application
deployment, and asset management. MuleSoft Platform MCP Server supports service discovery, policy
enforcement, and traffic monitoring across your organization.

## MuleSoft DX MCP Server

MuleSoft DX MCP Server provides structured, permission-scoped access to Anypoint Platform from AI
assistants in your IDE to help you design, build, publish, deploy, and secure MuleSoft assets by
turning natural-language goals into production-ready actions.

Typical lifecycle areas you address with MuleSoft DX MCP Server include:

- API specification development
- Application creation and configuration
- Agent network creation
- Asset publication in Exchange
- Instance deployment and management to CloudHub 2.0 and Runtime Fabric
- Application security

[Getting Started with MuleSoft DX MCP Server](getting-started)

## MuleSoft Platform MCP Server

MuleSoft Platform MCP Server provides governed access to your entire IT landscape, including APIs,
agents, MCP servers, and LLMs, to accelerate production-ready AI at scale. It helps you scan,
discover, govern, and monitor services across the infrastructure you already run.

Typical platform areas you address with MuleSoft Platform MCP Server include:

- Service scanning across agentic platforms
- Discovery of APIs, agents, MCP servers, and LLMs in one place
- Policy enforcement for PII detection, semantic routing, and token controls
- Monitoring of traffic, cost, and performance across services

[Getting Started with MuleSoft Platform MCP Server](getting-started-platform)
---

# MuleSoft MCP Servers Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft MCP Servers implement the Model Context Protocol (MCP) so AI assistants in your IDE can
work with MuleSoft products and services. MuleSoft DX MCP Server supports API design, application
deployment, and asset management. MuleSoft Platform MCP Server supports service discovery, policy
enforcement, and traffic monitoring across your organization.

## June 2, 2026

### Whatâs New

- New tool: `uc_get_test_generation_info`. Get information about which connector modules (CCM, ACM,
  FCM) are available and whether tests already exist before generating tests.
- New tool: `uc_generate_tests`. Generate tests for connector modules using either mock or real
  connections.
- Tool rename: `uc_generate_api_spec` is renamed to `uc_manage_api_spec`. The renamed tool also
  supports research-only and validation modes.
- Tool rename: `uc_publish_connector` is renamed to `uc_connector_lifecycle`. The renamed tool
  supports both `build` and `publish` actions.
- Tool update: `uc_generate_connector`. The `phases` parameter now accepts `flow` to generate a Flow
  Connector module in addition to the existing `ccm` and `anypoint` phases.

For tool parameter details, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools).

## May 28, 2026

### Whatâs New

- Introducing MuleSoft Platform MCP Server

  Get governed access to your entire IT landscape, including APIs, agents, MCP servers, and LLMs,
  and accelerate production-ready AI at scale. With remote access, use MuleSoft Platform MCP Server
  to scan, discover, govern, and monitor services across the infrastructure you already run. Typical
  platform areas you can address include:
  - Service scanning across agentic platforms
  - Discovery of APIs, agents, MCP servers, and LLMs in one place
  - Policy enforcement for PII detection, semantic routing, and token controls
  - Monitoring of traffic, cost, and performance across services

For more information, see
[Getting Started with MuleSoft Platform MCP Server](getting-started-platform)

- With the addition of MuleSoft Platform MCP Server, MuleSoft MCP Server is renamed to MuleSoft DX
  MCP Server. This is a non-breaking change and only cosmetically affects the UI and documentation.
  There are no changes to tool names.

## March 16, 2026

### Whatâs New

- Tool update: `get_flex_gateway_policy_example`. You can now generate examples for these policy
  features.
  - `ip_filter`: Allow or block requests based on IP addresses and CIDR ranges
  - `jwt_generate`: Generate JSON Web Tokens
  - `oauth2_token_introspection`: Validate OAuth2 tokens
  - `rate_limiting`: Allow or block requests based on rate limits
  - `data_storage`: Store and retrieve data using the Data Storage Library

- Installation namespace change: When installing MuleSoft MCP Server in your IDE, the installation
  command is now `npm install -g mulesoft-mcp-server`. Previously, it used `mulesoft/mcp-server`.

## March 6, 2026

### Whatâs New

- New tool: `uc_add_pagination`. Add pagination support to a specific operation.
- New tool: `uc_list_connection_profiles`. List all available connection profiles with their IDs,
  names, authentication types, and descriptions.
- New tool: `uc_manage_operations`. Manage connector operations.
- New tool: `uc_publish_connector`. Publish a connector to Anypoint Exchange.

For tool parameter details, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools).

## February 17, 2026

Tool update: `search_asset`. Use the new `exchangeScope` parameter to set the search scope for
either private or public assets. Additionally, the `search_asset` tool responses now use
standardized relevance scores.

## February 6, 2026

### Whatâs New

- Tool update: `implement_api_spec`. Use APIkit flows to implement an API specification in a new
  integration project. You can implement a specification from a local asset or an Anypoint Exchange
  asset.
- New tool: `generate_or_modify_munit_test`. Generate, modify, or fix MUnit tests for Mule flows.
  Provide the flow XML, test scenario description, and dependencies for accurate test generation.
  Previously generating and modifying MUnit tests were different tools.

For tool parameter details, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools).

## January 15, 2026

### Whatâs New

- New tool: `implement_api_spec`. Use APIkit flows to implement an API specification in a new
  integration project. You can implement a specification from an Anypoint Exchange asset.
- New tool: `generate_munit_test`. Generate MUnit test cases for Mule flows, one test scenario at a
  time, with support for mocks, assertions, and dependencies.
- New tool: `modify_munit_test`. Modify or fix an existing MUnit test case, including updating
  assertions, mocks, payloads, and test logic.

For tool parameter details, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools).

## November 20, 2025

### Whatâs New

You can now use the MuleSoft MCP Server to build connectors with Connector Builder. These new MCP
tools are available for use in Connector Builder.

- `uc_create_connector_workflow`
- `uc_generate_api_spec`
- `uc_generate_connector`

For more information, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools) and
[Generate Base Connectivity with MCP Tools](../connector-builder/base-connectivity#generate-base-connectivity-with-mcp-tools).

## October 3, 2025

### Whatâs New

Build Agent Fabric agent networks, manage governance rulesets, and more with new tools. All tools
are available in MuleSoft MCP Server in Anypoint Code Builder; they arenât available for use in
other IDEs.

- Tools to create, edit, publish, and deploy agent networks for Agent Fabric solutions.
- Tools to list, add, and validate governance rulesets.
- Tools to open, mock, and validate API specs.
- Tool to get metadata and instructions for enabling topics and agent actions from an OpenAI spec.

For more information, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools).

## August 11, 2025

### Whatâs New

- New tool: `create_and_manage_metadata`. Define metadata for a flow component, attach metadata to
  Mule flow components, and update existing metadata. To define metadata, provide the Mule
  configuration file URI and sample data or schema. To apply metadata, specify existing metadata
  types. To update metadata, provide new schema or sample data.
- New tool: `test_connection`. Test a connector configuration for a Mule project. For example, test
  connectivity for a HTTP listener connection.

For more information, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools).

## July 17, 2025

### Whatâs New

- App Development
  - New tool: `create_api_spec`. Use AI to create an API specification. You must enable Agentforce
    in [Access Management](../access-management/).
  - New tool: `create_api_spec_project`. Create and scaffold an API specification or fragment
    project. You can specify the project name, location, and language (RAML, OAS, and so on).
  - New tool: `create_MCP_server`. Create a MuleSoft MCP server. This tool uses Anypoint Connector
    for MCP (MCP Connector) to handle requests for creating MCP servers.
  - New tool: `create_mule_project`. Create an integration project.
  - New tool: `generate_mule_flow`. Use AI to create a flow.
  - New tool: `run_local_mule_application`. Deploy and run your app locally.
  - New tool: `search_asset`. Search for an asset in an organization and Anypoint Exchange. Returns
    the first asset in your organization that matches input criteria. If the tool doesnât find any
    assets in your organization, then the tool returns assets in Anypoint Exchange that exactly or
    partially match `searchQuery`.

- App Management
  - New tool: `create_and_manage_api_instances`. Create and configure API instances in Anypoint API
    Manager.
  - New tool: `list_api_instances`. Get information for instances in an environment. You can also
    filter by an instance ID or label and include applied or available policies. The tool paginates
    results using the page parameter. Prompt tip: To get information for all instances, revise the
    results by using the page parameter.
  - New tool: `list_applications`. Get information about all apps in the environment that you
    specify. You can also specify an optional deployment name. Results are sorted by last update
    date and time, in descending order.
  - New tool: `update_mule_application`. Update a Mule app deployed to CloudHub 2.0 or Anypoint
    Runtime Fabric. Update the runtime version, configuration, and other app settings.

- Policy Management
  - New tool: `manage_api_instance_policy`. Apply a new policy to an API instance and update an
    existing policy configuration.
  - New tool: `manage_flex_gateway_policy_project`. Provide instructions to the LLM on how to manage
    an Anypoint Flex Gateway custom policy project. Instructions include how to create the project,
    set up the configuration, and build the project. The instructions also include how to publish a
    development version of the project and publish a release version of the project. See
    [Prerequisites for Omni Gateway Policy Development Kit (PDK)](../pdk/latest/policies-pdk-prerequisites).
  - New tool: `get_flex_gateway_policy_example`. Get an Anypoint Flex Gateway policy example to help
    you configure your Rust source code. You can get example policies that manipulate a requestâs
    header and body, make HTTP calls to external services, share data between requests and
    responses, reject requests, and perform sleep operations.

- Usage and Insights
  - New tool: `get_platform_insights`. See how your organization uses Anypoint Platform through
    operational insights. Insights include:
    - Usage trends for apps, such as flow count, message counts, and data throughput by business
      group and environment. Usage trends are for the current month. If itâs early in the month, you
      get usage for the last full month.
    - API and app call volume across consumers for the last 7 days.
    - API and app performance metrics such as errors and latency for the last 7 days.

  - New tool: `get_reuse_metrics`. Get visibility into how you reuse assets across your
    organization. Drive efficiency, consistency, and cost savings by reducing duplication. Includes
    sandbox and production environments. Metrics are a year-to-date snapshot.

For more information, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools)

## June 3, 2025

### Whatâs New

- App Development
  - New tool: `create_mule_project`. Create an integration project.
  - New tool: `generate_mule_flow`. Use AI to create a flow.
  - New tool: `run_local_mule_application`. Deploy and run your app locally.
  - New tool: `search_asset`. Find an Anypoint Exchange asset by name or other parameters that you
    specify. Returns the first asset that matches the input criteria.
  - New tool: `create_and_manage_assets`. Create an asset in Anypoint Exchange, update some Anypoint
    Exchange asset properties without republishing the asset, and delete an asset in Anypoint
    Exchange. Asset properties are the asset name, description, icon, contact name, and contact
    email.

- App Management
  - New tool: `deploy_mule_application`. Deploy your app to CloudHub 2.0 or Anypoint Runtime Fabric
    and run it. You can deploy from a local project or Anypoint Exchange asset. This tool supports
    security, high availability, and performance optimizations for deployments.
  - New tool: `list_applications`. Get information about all apps in the environment that you
    specify. You can also specify an optional deployment name. Results are sorted by last update
    date and time, in descending order.

For more information, see [MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools)
---

# MuleSoft Platform MCP Server Tool Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft Platform MCP Server exposes tools for managing APIs, agents, LLMs, MCP servers, governance
strategies, policies, gateways, observability, and cost optimization across Anypoint Platform. Each
tool accepts specific input parameters so assistants can search the portfolio, inspect resources,
and apply changes on your behalf. Use these tools from your IDE or another MCP client to automate
platform administration and surface operational insight.

See [Get Started with MuleSoft Platform MCP Server](getting-started-platform) for setup and
authentication, including the Connected App scopes that map to each permission tier.

## Command Summary

href="#list-business-groups">list_business_groups</a></p></td><td><p>read:organization</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="#fetch-guardrail-ruleset">fetch_guardrail_ruleset</a></p></td><td><p>apimanager:environment_viewer</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a
