## apply_policy_to_instance

Apply the selected policy to the target asset instance using the provided configuration data.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>api_instance_id</code></p></td><td><p>API instance ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_id</code></p></td><td><p>Asset ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_version</code></p></td><td><p>Asset version (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration_data</code></p></td><td><p>Configuration data (PolicyConfigurationData or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>disabled</code></p></td><td><p>Disabled (boolean or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>group_id</code></p></td><td><p>Group ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>order</code></p></td><td><p>Order (integer or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>pointcut_data</code></p></td><td><p>Pointcut data (PolicyPointcutData or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>policy_template_id</code></p></td><td><p>Policy template ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "apply_policy_to_instance",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "environment_id": "<environment_id>",
    "api_instance_id": "<api_instance_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "policyId": "p_4f8a91d2",
  "apiInstanceId": "16834521",
  "policyTemplateId": "client-id-enforcement",
  "assetId": "client-id-enforcement",
  "assetVersion": "1.3.3",
  "order": 1,
  "disabled": false,
  "status": "applied"
}
```

## check_guardrail_scope_targets

Preview APIs matched by a guardrail scope filter.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>scope_filter</code></p></td><td><p>Scope filter (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "check_guardrail_scope_targets",
  "arguments": {
    "scope_filter": "apis:*"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "scopeFilter": "apis:*",
  "matchedCount": 47,
  "matches": [
    { "assetId": "orders-api", "assetVersion": "2.1.0", "name": "Orders API" },
    { "assetId": "payments-api", "assetVersion": "1.4.2", "name": "Payments API" },
    { "assetId": "customers-api", "assetVersion": "3.0.1", "name": "Customers API" }
  ],
  "truncated": true
}
```

## check_policy_conformance

Convert the selected policy application payload to JSON-LD and print it in backend logs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>api_instance_id</code></p></td><td><p>API instance ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_id</code></p></td><td><p>Asset ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_version</code></p></td><td><p>Asset version (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration_data</code></p></td><td><p>Configuration data (PolicyConfigurationData or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>disabled</code></p></td><td><p>Disabled (boolean or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>group_id</code></p></td><td><p>Group ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>order</code></p></td><td><p>Order (integer or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>pointcut_data</code></p></td><td><p>Pointcut data (PolicyPointcutData or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>policy_template_id</code></p></td><td><p>Policy template ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "check_policy_conformance",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "environment_id": "<environment_id>",
    "api_instance_id": "<api_instance_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "apiInstanceId": "16834521",
  "policyTemplateId": "client-id-enforcement",
  "conformant": true,
  "violations": [],
  "jsonLd": {
    "@context": "https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/governance/policy/v1",
    "@type": "PolicyApplication",
    "policy": "client-id-enforcement",
    "target": "16834521"
  }
}
```

## create_automated_policy_strategy

Create a new automated-policy governance strategy from wizard inputs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>allow_duplicated</code></p></td><td><p>Allow duplicated (boolean).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>automated_policy_configuration</code></p></td><td><p>Automated policy configuration (object or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>automated_policy_endpoint_type_groups</code></p></td><td><p>Automated policy endpoint type groups (array or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>automated_policy_endpoint_type_mode</code></p></td><td><p>Automated policy endpoint type mode (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>automated_policy_environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Automated policy environment ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>automated_policy_id</code></p></td><td><p>Automated policy ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>automated_policy_runtime</code></p></td><td><p>Automated policy runtime (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>automated_policy_runtime_range_from</code></p></td><td><p>Automated policy runtime range from (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>automated_policy_runtime_range_to</code></p></td><td><p>Automated policy runtime range to (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>Description (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>Name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>scope_filter</code></p></td><td><p>Scope filter (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "create_automated_policy_strategy",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "name": "Auto-apply rate limiting"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "strategyId": "strat_a91f2c4d",
  "strategyType": "automated-policy",
  "name": "Auto-apply rate limiting",
  "status": "active",
  "createdDate": "2026-05-07T14:32:18.412Z"
}
```

## create_guardrail

Create a new guardrail strategy from wizard inputs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>Description (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>governance_target_type</code></p></td><td><p>Governance target type (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>Name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>position</code></p></td><td><p>Position (integer or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>rules</code></p></td><td><p>Rules (array or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>rulesets</code></p></td><td><p>Rulesets (array or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>schedule_config</code></p></td><td><p>Schedule config (object or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>scope_condition_mode</code></p></td><td><p>Scope condition mode (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>scope_conditions</code></p></td><td><p>Scope conditions (array or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>scope_filter</code></p></td><td><p>Scope filter (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>strategy_status</code></p></td><td><p>Strategy status (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>strategy_type</code></p></td><td><p>Strategy type (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "create_guardrail",
  "arguments": {
    "description": "",
    "governance_target_type": "service-assets",
    "name": "<name>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "guardrailId": "gr_72bd1e3a",
  "name": "PII redaction in OpenAPI specs",
  "governanceTargetType": "service-assets",
  "scopeFilter": "apis:*",
  "rulesets": ["openapi-best-practices", "pii-redaction"],
  "status": "active",
  "createdDate": "2026-05-07T14:32:18.412Z"
}
```

## create_omni_gateway

Launch the managed gateway creation wizard for the active MuleSoft provider. When fully configured,
creates the gateway in Anypoint Platform.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment_name</code></p></td><td><p>Environment name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment_type</code></p></td><td><p>Environment type (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>governance_override_warnings</code></p></td><td><p>Governance override warnings (boolean).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>payload</code></p></td><td><p>Payload (FlexGatewayCreatePayload or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider_id</code></p></td><td><p>Provider ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>target_type</code></p></td><td><p>Target type (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "create_omni_gateway",
  "arguments": {
    "environment_id": "<environment_id>",
    "environment_name": "<environment_name>",
    "environment_type": "<environment_type>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "gatewayId": "fg_8c4e92a1",
  "name": "prod-omni-gateway",
  "environmentId": "e1f3b920-4c8a-11ee-be56-0242ac120002",
  "targetType": "cloudhub2",
  "status": "creating",
  "ingressDomains": ["prod-omni-gateway.us-e1.cloudhub.io"],
  "createdDate": "2026-05-07T14:32:18.412Z"
}
```

## create_scanner

Launch the scanner creation wizard to configure and create a new agent scanner. The wizard will
guide you through provider selection, authentication, and configuration. On success, returns the
created scanner details including scannerId. Pass organization_id when calling this tool. Call
WITHOUT name/provider to display the interactive wizard.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>auth_fields</code></p></td><td><p>Auth fields (ScannerAuthFields or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>auth_fields_alias</code></p></td><td><p>Auth fields alias (ScannerAuthFields or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>auth_method</code></p></td><td><p>Auth method (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>auth_method_alias</code></p></td><td><p>Auth method alias (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>Description (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>filters</code></p></td><td><p>Filters (array or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>Name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>notifications</code></p></td><td><p>Notifications (ScannerNotifications or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>owner_id</code></p></td><td><p>Owner ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider</code></p></td><td><p>Provider (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>schedule</code></p></td><td><p>Schedule (ScannerSchedule or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "create_scanner",
  "arguments": {
    "auth_fields": {},
    "auth_fields_alias": {},
    "auth_method": "<auth_method>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "scannerId": "sc_3d7a4f81",
  "name": "vertex-prod-scanner",
  "provider": "google-vertex",
  "authMethod": "service-account",
  "status": "active",
  "schedule": { "frequency": "daily", "hour": 2 },
  "createdDate": "2026-05-07T14:32:18.412Z"
}
```

## delete_automated_policy_strategy

Delete one automated-policy governance strategy by strategy ID.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>strategy_id</code></p></td><td><p>Strategy ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "delete_automated_policy_strategy",
  "arguments": {
    "strategy_id": "<strategy_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "strategyId": "strat_a91f2c4d",
  "deleted": true
}
```

## delete_governance_strategy

Delete one governance strategy by strategy id.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>strategy_id</code></p></td><td><p>Strategy ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "delete_governance_strategy",
  "arguments": {
    "strategy_id": "<strategy_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "strategyId": "gs_5e2f9a18",
  "deleted": true
}
```

## fetch_automated_policy_template

Fetch one policy template by templateId for configuration details.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>template_id</code></p></td><td><p>Template ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_automated_policy_template",
  "arguments": {
    "template_id": "<template_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "templateId": "rate-limiting",
  "name": "Rate Limiting",
  "category": "Quality of Service",
  "version": "1.4.0",
  "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "configurationSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "rateLimits": { "type": "array" },
      "exposeHeaders": { "type": "boolean" }
    }
  }
}
```

## fetch_cost_instance_timeseries

Use this tool to get token usage and response time timeseries for a single MCP server instance.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>instance_id</code></p></td><td><p>Instance ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>time_range</code></p></td><td><p>Time range (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_cost_instance_timeseries",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "instance_id": "<instance_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "instanceId": "16834521",
  "timeRange": "7d",
  "buckets": [
    {
      "timestamp": "2026-04-30T00:00:00Z",
      "totalTokens": 142318,
      "tokenDelta": 21405,
      "avgResponseTimeMs": 312
    },
    {
      "timestamp": "2026-05-01T00:00:00Z",
      "totalTokens": 158902,
      "tokenDelta": 24817,
      "avgResponseTimeMs": 298
    },
    {
      "timestamp": "2026-05-02T00:00:00Z",
      "totalTokens": 134210,
      "tokenDelta": 19503,
      "avgResponseTimeMs": 305
    }
  ]
}
```

## fetch_cost_instances

Use this tool to get per-instance token usage with optimization coverage.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>filter_unoptimized</code></p></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to return only instances that have unapplied cost policies (<code>unappliedCostPolicyIds</code> not empty); use this to find optimization candidates quickly.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID to query for per-instance token usage and optimization coverage.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>sort_by</code></p></td><td><p>Sort field for results. Supported values: <code>totalTokens</code>, <code>tokenDelta</code>, <code>avgResponseTimeMs</code>, <code>totalCalls</code>, <code>tokensPerCall</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>time_range</code></p></td><td><p>Time window for usage analysis. Supported values: <code>1h</code>, <code>6h</code>, <code>24h</code>, <code>7d</code>, <code>30d</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>top_n</code></p></td><td><p>Maximum number of instances to return. Defaults to <code>10</code>; maximum is <code>50</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_cost_instances",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "instances": [
    {
      "instanceId": "16834521",
      "instanceName": "orders-mcp-prod",
      "assetId": "orders-mcp-server",
      "environmentId": "e1f3b920-4c8a-11ee-be56-0242ac120002",
      "totalTokens": 4218903,
      "tokenDelta": 612418,
      "avgResponseTimeMs": 287,
      "totalCalls": 5142,
      "tokensPerCall": 820,
      "toolsListTokens": 1402189,
      "toolsCallTokens": 2816714,
      "appliedPolicies": ["client-id-enforcement"],
      "appliedCostPolicyIds": [],
      "unappliedCostPolicyIds": ["tool-output-truncation", "response-caching"],
      "optimizationCoverage": 0.0
    }
  ],
  "policyTemplates": {
    "tool-output-truncation": {
      "name": "Tool Output Truncation",
      "description": "Caps tool response token usage."
    },
    "response-caching": {
      "name": "Response Caching",
      "description": "Caches identical tool calls."
    }
  },
  "total": 18
}
```

## fetch_cost_optimization_recommendations

Use this tool to get a ranked list of MCP server instances that should be optimized, mirroring the
'Recommended Optimizations' card on the Cost Management page. Use this tool to answer questions,
such as "What should I optimize next?', "What are the biggest token savings opportunities?"", "Where
is the most waste?".

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID to query for optimization recommendations.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>savings_pool_size</code></p></td><td><p>Number of candidate instances to evaluate before ranking the final recommendations; increase this when you want broader coverage before taking the top results.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>time_range</code></p></td><td><p>Time window used to detect active instances and estimate projected token savings. Supported values: <code>1h</code>, <code>6h</code>, <code>24h</code>, <code>7d</code>, <code>30d</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>top_n</code></p></td><td><p>Maximum number of ranked recommendations to return. Defaults to <code>3</code>; maximum is <code>10</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_cost_optimization_recommendations",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "instanceId": "16834521",
      "instanceName": "orders-mcp-prod",
      "serverName": "Orders MCP Server",
      "assetId": "orders-mcp-server",
      "environment": "production",
      "totalTokens": 4218903,
      "projectedTokensSaved": 1180294,
      "projectedSavingsPercent": 27.97,
      "unappliedCostPolicyIds": ["tool-output-truncation", "response-caching"],
      "perPolicyProjections": [
        {
          "policyId": "tool-output-truncation",
          "policyName": "Tool Output Truncation",
          "rate": 0.18,
          "tokensSaved": 759402
        },
        {
          "policyId": "response-caching",
          "policyName": "Response Caching",
          "rate": 0.1,
          "tokensSaved": 420892
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "savingsPoolSize": 3,
  "timeRange": "30d"
}
```

## fetch_cost_overview

Use this tool to get org-wide token usage KPIs for MCP servers: total tokens, saved tokens (from
optimization policies), and total tool calls.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>include_timeseries</code></p></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to include token usage timeseries for charting; this increases response size.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID to query for org-wide MCP token usage KPIs.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>time_range</code></p></td><td><p>Time window for KPI aggregation. Supported values: <code>1h</code>, <code>6h</code>, <code>24h</code>, <code>7d</code>, <code>30d</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_cost_overview",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "timeRange": "30d",
  "kpis": {
    "totalTokens": 18472310,
    "savedTokens": 3214087,
    "totalToolCalls": 24158,
    "tokensPerCall": 765,
    "savingsPercent": 14.8
  },
  "timeseries": [
    { "timestamp": "2026-04-08T00:00:00Z", "totalTokens": 612430, "savedTokens": 98215 },
    { "timestamp": "2026-04-09T00:00:00Z", "totalTokens": 587102, "savedTokens": 104812 }
  ]
}
```

## fetch_governance_service_report

Use this tool to get detailed governance conformance data for a single service.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>api_version</code></p></td><td><p>Optional API version to scope the report when a service has multiple versions.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>include_rule_messages</code></p></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to include per-rule conformance messages in the response.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID that owns the service.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>service_id</code></p></td><td><p>Service ID to retrieve detailed governance conformance for.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>version</code></p></td><td><p>Optional service version selector (legacy alias). Use when you need a specific versionâs conformance view.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_governance_service_report",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "service_id": "<service_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "service": {
    "serviceId": "orders-api",
    "name": "Orders API",
    "provider": "mulesoft",
    "version": "2.1.0",
    "conformanceStatus": "non-compliant"
  },
  "rulesets": [
    {
      "rulesetId": "openapi-best-practices",
      "name": "OpenAPI Best Practices",
      "errorCount": 2,
      "warningCount": 5,
      "infoCount": 1,
      "rules": [
        {
          "ruleId": "operation-success-response",
          "severity": "error",
          "message": "Operation 'POST /orders' is missing a 2xx response."
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "instanceConformance": [
    { "instanceId": "16834521", "environment": "production", "conformant": false }
  ]
}
```

## fetch_governance_services

Use this tool to search, filter, and count governance conformance across services.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>concern_area_ids</code></p></td><td><p>Filter to one or more concern area IDs (for example <code>security-privacy</code>, <code>consumption-economic-governance</code>, <code>structural-conformity-lifecycle</code>).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>conformance_status_in</code></p></td><td><p>Filter to one or more conformance states. Canonical values: <code>compliant</code>, <code>non-compliant</code>, <code>pending</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>include_rule_messages</code></p></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to include rule-level messages in each returned service row.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>include_rulesets</code></p></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to include violated ruleset details in each service row.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>limit</code></p></td><td><p>Page size for results.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>min_errors</code></p></td><td><p>Minimum error count threshold for matched services.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>min_infos</code></p></td><td><p>Minimum info count threshold for matched services.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>min_warnings</code></p></td><td><p>Minimum warning count threshold for matched services.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>offset</code></p></td><td><p>Result offset for pagination.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID to search for governance conformance data.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider_in</code></p></td><td><p>Filter to one or more gateway/provider values. Canonical values include <code>mulesoft</code>, <code>kong</code>, <code>apigee</code>, <code>aws</code>, <code>azure</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>q</code></p></td><td><p>Free-text search across service metadata.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>ruleset_ids_in</code></p></td><td><p>Filter to services associated with one or more ruleset IDs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>service_ids</code></p></td><td><p>Filter to specific service IDs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>service_type_in</code></p></td><td><p>Filter to one or more service types.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_governance_services",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "services": [
    {
      "serviceId": "orders-api",
      "name": "Orders API",
      "provider": "mulesoft",
      "serviceType": "rest-api",
      "conformanceStatus": "non-compliant",
      "errorCount": 2,
      "warningCount": 5,
      "violatedRulesets": ["openapi-best-practices"]
    },
    {
      "serviceId": "payments-api",
      "name": "Payments API",
      "provider": "mulesoft",
      "serviceType": "rest-api",
      "conformanceStatus": "compliant",
      "errorCount": 0,
      "warningCount": 0,
      "violatedRulesets": []
    }
  ],
  "totals": {
    "statusTotals": { "compliant": 38, "non-compliant": 9, "pending": 2 },
    "concernTotals": {
      "security-privacy": { "errorCount": 4, "warningCount": 11 },
      "structural-conformity-lifecycle": { "errorCount": 2, "warningCount": 5 }
    },
    "providerTotals": {
      "mulesoft": { "compliant": 32, "non-compliant": 7, "pending": 1 },
      "kong": { "compliant": 6, "non-compliant": 2, "pending": 1 }
    }
  },
  "total": 49,
  "limit": 25,
  "offset": 0
}
```

## fetch_guardrail_ruleset

Fetch a guardrail ruleset payload by sourceRulesetKey.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>source_ruleset_key</code></p></td><td><p>Source ruleset key (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_guardrail_ruleset",
  "arguments": {
    "source_ruleset_key": "<source_ruleset_key>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "sourceRulesetKey": "openapi-best-practices",
  "name": "OpenAPI Best Practices",
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "rules": [
    {
      "ruleId": "operation-success-response",
      "severity": "error",
      "description": "Operations should declare a successful 2xx response."
    },
    {
      "ruleId": "operation-tag-defined",
      "severity": "warning",
      "description": "Operations should be tagged for grouping."
    }
  ]
}
```

## fetch_monitoring_drill_down

Use this tool to rank services or instances contributing to a specific performance metric
(availability, reliability, avgLatency, requestVolume, errorRate, or policyViolations). Use this
tool to answer queries such as, "Which services are driving errors?" or "Rank instances by latency."

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>entity_type</code></p></td><td><p>Optional entity category to rank. Supported values: <code>apis</code>, <code>agents</code>, <code>mcp-servers</code>, <code>llms</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment</code></p></td><td><p>Optional single environment filter (legacy convenience field).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environments</code></p></td><td><p>Optional list of environments to filter contributors by.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>instance_id</code></p></td><td><p>Optional instance ID to narrow results to a single instance.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>limit</code></p></td><td><p>Page size for ranked contributors.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metric</code></p></td><td><p>Metric used for ranking. Supported values: <code>availability</code>, <code>reliability</code>, <code>avgLatency</code>, <code>requestVolume</code>, <code>errorRate</code>, <code>policyViolations</code>.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>offset</code></p></td><td><p>Result offset for pagination.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Optional organization ID scope (required in multi-org contexts).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>providers</code></p></td><td><p>Optional list of provider filters.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>search</code></p></td><td><p>Free-text filter matching service, instance, or environment names.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>service_id</code></p></td><td><p>Optional service ID to narrow the ranking to one serviceâs instances.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>sort_dir</code></p></td><td><p>Sort direction for ranked results, typically <code>asc</code> or <code>desc</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>sort_field</code></p></td><td><p>Field to sort by in the drill-down result set.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>time_range</code></p></td><td><p>Time window for metric calculation (for example <code>24h</code>).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_monitoring_drill_down",
  "arguments": {
    "metric": "availability"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "metric": "availability",
  "rows": [
    {
      "serviceId": "orders-api",
      "instanceId": "16834521",
      "name": "Orders API",
      "environment": "production",
      "value": 99.92,
      "delta": -0.04
    },
    {
      "serviceId": "payments-api",
      "instanceId": "16834522",
      "name": "Payments API",
      "environment": "production",
      "value": 99.81,
      "delta": 0.01
    },
    {
      "serviceId": "customers-api",
      "instanceId": "16834523",
      "name": "Customers API",
      "environment": "production",
      "value": 99.43,
      "delta": -0.21
    }
  ],
  "totalRows": 12,
  "offset": 0,
  "pageSize": 10
}
```

## fetch_monitoring_instance

Use this tool to get monitoring data for a single API instance: time-series charts (requests, error
rate, latency, policy violations), top endpoints by traffic, and top clients by traffic.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>api_version</code></p></td><td><p>Optional API version selector for the target asset.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_id</code></p></td><td><p>API asset ID to retrieve instance monitoring for.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_version</code></p></td><td><p>Optional asset version selector.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment</code></p></td><td><p>Target environment for monitoring data. Supported values: <code>production</code>, <code>sandbox</code>, <code>development</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>instance_id</code></p></td><td><p>Optional instance ID. If omitted, the first available instance is selected.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID that owns the API asset and instance.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>time_range</code></p></td><td><p>Time window for charts and summaries. Supported values: <code>1h</code>, <code>6h</code>, <code>24h</code>, <code>7d</code>, <code>30d</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_monitoring_instance",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "asset_id": "<asset_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "instanceId": "16834521",
  "assetId": "orders-api",
  "environment": "production",
  "timeRange": "24h",
  "charts": {
    "requests": [{ "timestamp": "2026-05-07T13:00:00Z", "value": 4218 }],
    "errorRate": [{ "timestamp": "2026-05-07T13:00:00Z", "value": 0.012 }],
    "avgLatency": [{ "timestamp": "2026-05-07T13:00:00Z", "value": 287 }],
    "policyViolations": [{ "timestamp": "2026-05-07T13:00:00Z", "value": 0 }]
  },
  "topEndpoints": [
    { "path": "/orders", "method": "POST", "requests": 18420 },
    { "path": "/orders/{id}", "method": "GET", "requests": 12508 }
  ],
  "topClients": [{ "clientId": "c_4f8a91d2", "clientName": "checkout-web", "requests": 21408 }]
}
```

## fetch_monitoring_overview

Use this tool to get an org-wide monitoring health snapshot: total requests, error rate, average
latency, availability, reliability, and compact trend charts.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>environment</code></p></td><td><p>Environment scope for KPI and trend data. Supported values: <code>production</code>, <code>sandbox</code>, <code>development</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>include_charts</code></p></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to include compact time-series chart data with the KPI summary.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID to query for org-wide monitoring health metrics.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider</code></p></td><td><p>Optional provider filter. Monitoring data is currently available for <code>mulesoft</code>; other canonical providers (<code>kong</code>, <code>apigee</code>, <code>aws</code>, <code>azure</code>) return empty results.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>time_range</code></p></td><td><p>Time window for KPI and trend calculations. Supported values: <code>1h</code>, <code>6h</code>, <code>24h</code>, <code>7d</code>, <code>30d</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_monitoring_overview",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "environment": "production",
  "provider": "mulesoft",
  "timeRange": "7d",
  "kpis": {
    "totalRequests": 9128342,
    "errorRate": 0.018,
    "avgLatencyMs": 312,
    "availability": 99.87,
    "reliability": 99.41
  },
  "charts": {
    "requestVolume": [{ "timestamp": "2026-05-01T00:00:00Z", "value": 1284018 }],
    "errorRate": [{ "timestamp": "2026-05-01T00:00:00Z", "value": 0.021 }],
    "avgLatency": [{ "timestamp": "2026-05-01T00:00:00Z", "value": 305 }],
    "errorVolume": [{ "timestamp": "2026-05-01T00:00:00Z", "value": 26904 }]
  }
}
```

## fetch_service_facets

Use this tool when you need aggregate counts/buckets for summaries, dashboards, or filter panels,
not full service-row hydration.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>aggregates</code></p></td><td><p>Facet buckets/metrics to compute (for example <code>totalHits</code>, <code>byType</code>, <code>byProvider</code>). Use structured aggregate fields; do not send shorthand strings like <code>type,provider</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_types</code></p></td><td><p>Optional typed asset filter. Canonical values include API classifiers (<code>rest-api</code>, <code>http-api</code>, <code>graphql</code>, <code>soap-api</code>, <code>grpc-api</code>, <code>evented-api</code>) plus <code>agent</code> and <code>mcp</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>business_group_id</code></p></td><td><p>Business group ID to scope the facet query.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>filters</code></p></td><td><p>Optional structured typed filters. Do not combine with <code>q</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>q</code></p></td><td><p>Optional text query mode. When provided, do not send typed <code>filters</code> or <code>aggregates</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>size</code></p></td><td><p>Optional limit for returned facet buckets/results.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_service_facets",
  "arguments": {
    "business_group_id": "<business_group_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "businessGroupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "total": 142,
  "aggregates": {
    "byType": {
      "rest-api": 87,
      "graphql": 12,
      "soap-api": 18,
      "agent": 14,
      "mcp": 11
    },
    "byProvider": {
      "mulesoft": 118,
      "kong": 15,
      "apigee": 9
    },
    "byStatus": {
      "active": 128,
      "deprecated": 9,
      "draft": 5
    }
  }
}
```

## fetch_services

Use this tool when you need concrete service rows (for example asset IDs/names, detail selection, or
navigation affordances).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>aggregates</code></p></td><td><p>Optional aggregate blocks to return alongside service rows (for example counts by type/provider). Use structured aggregate fields, not shorthand strings.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_types</code></p></td><td><p>Optional typed asset filter. Canonical values include API classifiers (<code>rest-api</code>, <code>http-api</code>, <code>graphql</code>, <code>soap-api</code>, <code>grpc-api</code>, <code>evented-api</code>) plus <code>agent</code> and <code>mcp</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>business_group_id</code></p></td><td><p>Business group ID to scope the service search.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>filters</code></p></td><td><p>Optional structured typed filters. Do not combine with <code>q</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>include_affordances</code></p></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to include navigation/action affordances in each service row.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>q</code></p></td><td><p>Optional text query mode. When provided, do not send typed <code>filters</code> or <code>aggregates</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>size</code></p></td><td><p>Page size for returned service rows.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "fetch_services",
  "arguments": {
    "business_group_id": "<business_group_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "businessGroupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "total": 142,
  "services": [
    {
      "assetId": "orders-api",
      "name": "Orders API",
      "assetType": "rest-api",
      "provider": "mulesoft",
      "version": "2.1.0",
      "status": "active",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
      "links": { "self": "/exchange/api/v2/assets/orders-api/2.1.0" }
    },
    {
      "assetId": "payments-api",
      "name": "Payments API",
      "assetType": "rest-api",
      "provider": "mulesoft",
      "version": "1.4.2",
      "status": "active",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
      "links": { "self": "/exchange/api/v2/assets/payments-api/1.4.2" }
    }
  ]
}
```

## get_business_group

Get full metadata for a business group by its ID.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>group_id</code></p></td><td><p>Group ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "get_business_group",
  "arguments": {
    "group_id": "<group_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "id": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "name": "Acme Corp",
  "parentId": null,
  "isMaster": true,
  "ownerId": "u-9f2a8c12",
  "subOrganizationIds": ["b21d4f88-1c5e-4a3d-9e7b-0a4c6b3e8d11"],
  "entitlements": {
    "vcoreProduction": 50,
    "vcoreSandbox": 10
  }
}
```

## get_mcp_server_state

Read the deployed state of a catalog-managed MCP server. Returns current systems, tools, and
upstream IDs needed for editing.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>asset_id</code></p></td><td><p>Asset ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "get_mcp_server_state",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "environment_id": "<environment_id>",
    "asset_id": "<asset_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "assetId": "orders-mcp-server",
  "apiId": 16834521,
  "serverName": "Orders MCP Server",
  "targetId": "fg_8c4e92a1",
  "targetName": "prod-omni-gateway",
  "basePath": "/mcp/orders",
  "deployedSystems": [
    {
      "systemId": "salesforce",
      "tools": ["query", "create-record", "update-record"],
      "upstreamId": "u_2f1a8e44"
    }
  ],
  "currentUpstreamIds": { "salesforce": "u_2f1a8e44" }
}
```

## get_omni_gateway_target_domains

Get ingress domains and app unique id for a managed Omni Gateway target.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider_id</code></p></td><td><p>Provider ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>target_id</code></p></td><td><p>Target ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "get_omni_gateway_target_domains",
  "arguments": {
    "environment_id": "<environment_id>",
    "target_id": "<target_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "targetId": "fg_8c4e92a1",
  "appUniqueId": "prod-omni-gateway-us-east-1",
  "ingressDomains": ["prod-omni-gateway.us-e1.cloudhub.io", "api.acme.com"]
}
```

## get_omni_gateway_usage_report

Get current managed Omni Gateway size entitlement usage.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider_id</code></p></td><td><p>Provider ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "get_omni_gateway_usage_report",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "provider_id": "<provider_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "providerId": "p_mulesoft_default",
  "entitlements": {
    "totalSize": "Medium",
    "usedReplicas": 4,
    "totalReplicas": 10
  },
  "byEnvironment": [
    {
      "environmentId": "e1f3b920-4c8a-11ee-be56-0242ac120002",
      "name": "production",
      "replicas": 3
    },
    { "environmentId": "e2a4c731-1f9b-4c20-9e43-7a8e22b14d09", "name": "sandbox", "replicas": 1 }
  ]
}
```

## get_policy_template_form

Load one policy template detail, including configuration schema, to dynamically render the creation
form. Use this immediately after the user selects a policy in prepare_policy_creation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>api_instance_id</code></p></td><td><p>API instance ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_id</code></p></td><td><p>Asset ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_version</code></p></td><td><p>Asset version (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>direction</code></p></td><td><p>Direction (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>group_id</code></p></td><td><p>Group ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>template_id</code></p></td><td><p>Template ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "get_policy_template_form",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "environment_id": "<environment_id>",
    "api_instance_id": "<api_instance_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "templateId": "rate-limiting",
  "name": "Rate Limiting",
  "version": "1.4.0",
  "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "form": {
    "fields": [
      { "name": "rateLimits", "type": "array", "required": true, "label": "Rate Limits" },
      {
        "name": "exposeHeaders",
        "type": "boolean",
        "required": false,
        "label": "Expose Headers",
        "default": true
      }
    ]
  },
  "direction": "inbound"
}
```

## get_provision_status

Poll the status of a provisioning or update job. Returns status (running/complete/failed), progress
step, completed steps, result (MCP URL on success), and can_resume flag.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>job_id</code></p></td><td><p>Job ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "get_provision_status",
  "arguments": {
    "job_id": "<job_id>",
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "jobId": "job_a1b2c3d4",
  "status": "running",
  "progressStep": "deploying-flex-gateway",
  "completedSteps": ["create-asset", "configure-upstreams"],
  "remainingSteps": ["deploy-flex-gateway", "register-mcp-server"],
  "result": null,
  "canResume": false
}
```

## get_trusted_mcp_catalog

Return the catalog of SaaS systems available for MCP server provisioning. Each item includes tools,
auth type, credential fields, and upstream config.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "get_trusted_mcp_catalog",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "catalog": [
    {
      "systemId": "salesforce",
      "name": "Salesforce",
      "authType": "oauth2",
      "credentialFields": ["clientId", "clientSecret", "instanceUrl"],
      "tools": ["query", "create-record", "update-record", "delete-record"]
    },
    {
      "systemId": "github",
      "name": "GitHub",
      "authType": "personal-access-token",
      "credentialFields": ["token"],
      "tools": ["list-repos", "get-file", "create-issue", "list-pull-requests"]
    }
  ]
}
```

## list_agents

List agents registered in the Omni Platform for a given organization. Presents an interactive UI for
browsing, filtering, and selecting agents.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>Name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider</code></p></td><td><p>Provider (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "list_agents",
  "arguments": {
    "name": "<name>",
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "provider": "mulesoft"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "agents": [
    {
      "assetId": "invoice-approval-agent",
      "name": "Invoice Approval Agent",
      "version": "1.2.0",
      "provider": "mulesoft",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
      "description": "Routes invoices to approvers based on amount thresholds."
    },
    {
      "assetId": "support-triage-agent",
      "name": "Support Triage Agent",
      "version": "0.9.1",
      "provider": "mulesoft",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
      "description": "Classifies incoming support tickets."
    }
  ],
  "total": 14
}
```

## list_apis

List APIs from the Omni Platform catalog for a given organization. Presents an interactive UI for
browsing, filtering, and selecting APIs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>api_category</code></p></td><td><p>API category (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>Name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider</code></p></td><td><p>Provider (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "list_apis",
  "arguments": {
    "api_category": "<api_category>",
    "name": "<name>",
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "apis": [
    {
      "assetId": "orders-api",
      "name": "Orders API",
      "apiCategory": "Commerce",
      "version": "2.1.0",
      "provider": "mulesoft",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913"
    },
    {
      "assetId": "payments-api",
      "name": "Payments API",
      "apiCategory": "Commerce",
      "version": "1.4.2",
      "provider": "mulesoft",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913"
    }
  ],
  "total": 87
}
```

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>Name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

  "name": "list_business_groups",
  "arguments": {
    "name": "<name>"
  }
}
```

  "businessGroups": [
    {
      "id": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
      "name": "Acme Corp",
      "parentId": null,
      "isMaster": true
    },
    {
      "id": "b21d4f88-1c5e-4a3d-9e7b-0a4c6b3e8d11",
      "name": "Acme Payments Division",
      "parentId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
      "isMaster": false
    }
  ],
  "total": 2
}
```

## list_governance_strategies

List governance strategies for an organization. Shows the same searchable and filterable table style
as the main web app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>strategy_type</code></p></td><td><p>Strategy type (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "list_governance_strategies",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "strategy_type": "<strategy_type>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategyId": "strat_a91f2c4d",
      "name": "Auto-apply rate limiting",
      "strategyType": "automated-policy",
      "status": "active",
      "scopeFilter": "apis:*",
      "createdDate": "2026-04-12T08:21:43.000Z"
    },
    {
      "strategyId": "gs_5e2f9a18",
      "name": "PII redaction guardrail",
      "strategyType": "guardrail",
      "status": "active",
      "scopeFilter": "apis:tags:public",
      "createdDate": "2026-03-28T11:09:22.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 6
}
```

for browsing, filtering, and selecting LLMs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>category</code></p></td><td><p>Category (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>Name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider</code></p></td><td><p>Provider (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

  "arguments": {
    "category": "<category>",
    "name": "<name>",
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

{
  "llms": [
    {
      "assetId": "claude-opus-4-7",

      "category": "general-purpose",
      "provider": "anthropic",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913"

      "provider": "openai",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913"
    }
  ],
  "total": 9
}
```

## list_mcp_servers

List MCP servers registered in the Omni Platform for a given organization. Presents an interactive
UI for browsing, filtering, and selecting MCP servers.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>Name (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>protocol</code></p></td><td><p>Protocol (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "list_mcp_servers",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "mcpServers": [
    {
      "assetId": "orders-mcp-server",
      "name": "Orders MCP Server",
      "protocol": "streamable-http",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
      "instances": 2
    },
    {
      "assetId": "salesforce-mcp-server",
      "name": "Salesforce MCP Server",
      "protocol": "streamable-http",
      "version": "1.2.1",
      "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
      "instances": 3
    }
  ],
  "total": 11
}
```

## list_omni_gateway_environments

List Anypoint environments available for managed Omni Gateway creation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider_id</code></p></td><td><p>Provider ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "list_omni_gateway_environments",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "provider_id": "<provider_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "environments": [
    {
      "environmentId": "e1f3b920-4c8a-11ee-be56-0242ac120002",
      "name": "production",
      "type": "production",
      "isProduction": true
    },
    {
      "environmentId": "e2a4c731-1f9b-4c20-9e43-7a8e22b14d09",
      "name": "sandbox",
      "type": "sandbox",
      "isProduction": false
    },
    {
      "environmentId": "e3d2a811-7f5c-4983-89ce-a141f9120abc",
      "name": "development",
      "type": "design",
      "isProduction": false
    }
  ]
}
```

## list_omni_gateway_targets

List managed Omni Gateway deployment targets for an environment.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider_id</code></p></td><td><p>Provider ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "list_omni_gateway_targets",
  "arguments": {
    "environment_id": "<environment_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "targets": [
    {
      "targetId": "fg_8c4e92a1",
      "name": "prod-omni-gateway",
      "targetType": "cloudhub2",
      "status": "running",
      "ingressDomains": ["prod-omni-gateway.us-e1.cloudhub.io"],
      "environmentId": "e1f3b920-4c8a-11ee-be56-0242ac120002"
    }
  ]
}
```

## list_provider_gateways

List gateways for the active provider. Currently returns Flex Gateways when the active provider is
MuleSoft.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider_id</code></p></td><td><p>Provider ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "list_provider_gateways",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "provider_id": "<provider_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "providerId": "p_mulesoft_default",
  "gatewayType": "flex-gateway",
  "gateways": [
    {
      "gatewayId": "fg_8c4e92a1",
      "name": "prod-omni-gateway",
      "status": "running",
      "version": "1.9.2",
      "environmentId": "e1f3b920-4c8a-11ee-be56-0242ac120002"
    },
    {
      "gatewayId": "fg_2f1a8e44",
      "name": "sandbox-flex-gateway",
      "status": "running",
      "version": "1.9.2",
      "environmentId": "e2a4c731-1f9b-4c20-9e43-7a8e22b14d09"
    }
  ]
}
```

## list_scanner_providers

Get the list of available scanner providers (Google Vertex, Microsoft Copilot, and others).

This tool does not accept any input parameters.

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "list_scanner_providers",
  "arguments": {}
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "providers": [
    {
      "providerId": "google-vertex",
      "name": "Google Vertex AI",
      "authMethods": ["service-account", "oauth2"]
    },
    { "providerId": "microsoft-copilot", "name": "Microsoft Copilot", "authMethods": ["oauth2"] },
    {
      "providerId": "aws-bedrock",
      "name": "AWS Bedrock",
      "authMethods": ["iam-role", "access-key"]
    }
  ]
}
```

## login

Authenticate with Omni Platform. MUST be called before any other tool.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>password</code></p></td><td><p>Password (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>username</code></p></td><td><p>Username (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "login",
  "arguments": {
    "password": "<password>",
    "username": "<username>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "authenticated": true,
  "userId": "u-9f2a8c12",
  "username": "alice@acme.com",
  "activeOrganizationId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "expiresAt": "2026-05-08T14:32:18.412Z"
}
```

## logout

Clear the stored authentication token.

This tool does not accept any input parameters.

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "logout",
  "arguments": {}
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "authenticated": false
}
```

## prepare_automated_policy_creation

Open the automated-policy creation wizard with scope options and policy templates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "prepare_automated_policy_creation",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "scopeOptions": [
    { "scopeFilter": "apis:*", "label": "All APIs" },
    { "scopeFilter": "apis:tags:public", "label": "APIs tagged public" }
  ],
  "policyTemplates": [
    { "templateId": "rate-limiting", "name": "Rate Limiting", "category": "Quality of Service" },
    {
      "templateId": "client-id-enforcement",
      "name": "Client ID Enforcement",
      "category": "Security"
    },
    { "templateId": "jwt-validation", "name": "JWT Validation", "category": "Security" }
  ]
}
```

## prepare_guardrail_creation

Open the guardrail creation wizard with scope options and guardrail rule catalog data, using the
same scope, selection, and naming flow as the web app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "prepare_guardrail_creation",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "scopeOptions": [
    { "scopeFilter": "apis:*", "label": "All APIs" },
    { "scopeFilter": "apis:tags:public", "label": "APIs tagged public" }
  ],
  "rulesetCatalog": [
    { "rulesetId": "openapi-best-practices", "name": "OpenAPI Best Practices", "ruleCount": 24 },
    { "rulesetId": "pii-redaction", "name": "PII Redaction", "ruleCount": 8 },
    { "rulesetId": "owasp-api-top-10", "name": "OWASP API Top 10", "ruleCount": 10 }
  ]
}
```

## prepare_policy_creation

Load policy options for one asset instance.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>api_instance_id</code></p></td><td><p>API instance ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>asset_id</code></p></td><td><p>Asset ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>policy_name_hint</code></p></td><td><p>Policy name hint (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "prepare_policy_creation",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "apiInstanceId": "16834521",
  "assetId": "orders-api",
  "assetVersion": "2.1.0",
  "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "availablePolicies": [
    {
      "templateId": "rate-limiting",
      "assetId": "rate-limiting",
      "assetVersion": "1.4.0",
      "name": "Rate Limiting",
      "category": "Quality of Service"
    },
    {
      "templateId": "jwt-validation",
      "assetId": "jwt-validation",
      "assetVersion": "1.3.1",
      "name": "JWT Validation",
      "category": "Security"
    },
    {
      "templateId": "ip-allowlist",
      "assetId": "ip-allowlist",
      "assetVersion": "1.2.0",
      "name": "IP Allowlist",
      "category": "Security"
    }
  ]
}
```

## provision_mcp_server

Provision a composite MCP server on Flex Gateway from selected catalog systems.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>base_path</code></p></td><td><p>Base path (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment_id</code></p></td><td><p>Environment ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>server_name</code></p></td><td><p>Server name (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>servers</code></p></td><td><p>Servers (array).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>target_id</code></p></td><td><p>Target ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>target_name</code></p></td><td><p>Target name (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "provision_mcp_server",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "environment_id": "<environment_id>",
    "server_name": "<server_name>",
    "target_id": "<target_id>",
    "target_name": "<target_name>",
    "base_path": "/<base_path>",
    "servers": [{}]
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "jobId": "job_a1b2c3d4",
  "status": "running",
  "serverName": "Orders MCP Server",
  "basePath": "/mcp/orders",
  "targetId": "fg_8c4e92a1",
  "estimatedDurationSeconds": 90
}
```

## resume_provision_job

Resume a failed provisioning or update job from its last checkpoint. Only works on jobs with status
'failed' that have completed steps.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>job_id</code></p></td><td><p>Job ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "resume_provision_job",
  "arguments": {
    "job_id": "<job_id>",
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "jobId": "job_a1b2c3d4",
  "status": "running",
  "resumedFromStep": "deploy-flex-gateway"
}
```

## search_assets_semantic

Search portfolio assets using natural language semantic search. Use this when the user asks for
assets related to a concept or use case, not just keyword matching. Examples: 'Find services for
payment processing', 'Search for customer authentication services'. Returns asset names,
descriptions, relevance scores, and types.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>asset_type</code></p></td><td><p>Asset type (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organization_id</code></p></td><td><p>Organization ID (string or null).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>query</code></p></td><td><p>Query (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>rows</code></p></td><td><p>Rows (integer).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "search_assets_semantic",
  "arguments": {
    "query": "payment processing"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "query": "payment processing",
  "results": [
    {
      "assetId": "payments-api",
      "name": "Payments API",
      "assetType": "rest-api",
      "description": "Handles credit card and ACH payment authorization and capture.",
      "relevanceScore": 0.94,
      "version": "1.4.2"
    },
    {
      "assetId": "stripe-mcp-server",
      "name": "Stripe MCP Server",
      "assetType": "mcp",
      "description": "Tools for Stripe charges, refunds, and customer management.",
      "relevanceScore": 0.87,
      "version": "0.5.0"
    }
  ],
  "total": 6
}
```

## search_global_assets

Compatibility alias for global content search. Searches across agents, MCP servers, and APIs in one
business group. You must pass exactly one business_group_id.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>business_group_id</code></p></td><td><p>Business group ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>query</code></p></td><td><p>Query (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>size</code></p></td><td><p>Size (integer).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "search_global_assets",
  "arguments": {
    "query": "payment processing",
    "business_group_id": "<business_group_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "query": "payment processing",
  "businessGroupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "results": [
    {
      "assetId": "payments-api",
      "name": "Payments API",
      "assetType": "rest-api",
      "relevanceScore": 0.94
    },
    {
      "assetId": "stripe-mcp-server",
      "name": "Stripe MCP Server",
      "assetType": "mcp",
      "relevanceScore": 0.87
    },
    {
      "assetId": "fraud-detection-agent",
      "name": "Fraud Detection Agent",
      "assetType": "agent",
      "relevanceScore": 0.71
    }
  ],
  "total": 8
}
```

## search_global_content

UI tool for embedded agents to search content across agents, MCP servers, and APIs in the Omni
Platform.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>business_group_id</code></p></td><td><p>Business group ID (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>query</code></p></td><td><p>Query (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>size</code></p></td><td><p>Size (integer).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "search_global_content",
  "arguments": {
    "query": "payment processing",
    "business_group_id": "<business_group_id>"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "query": "payment processing",
  "businessGroupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
  "results": [
    {
      "assetId": "payments-api",
      "name": "Payments API",
      "assetType": "rest-api",
      "snippet": "Handles credit card and ACH payment authorization and capture.",
      "relevanceScore": 0.94
    },
    {
      "assetId": "stripe-mcp-server",
      "name": "Stripe MCP Server",
      "assetType": "mcp",
      "snippet": "Tools for Stripe charges, refunds, and customer management.",
      "relevanceScore": 0.87
    }
  ],
  "total": 8
}
```

## search_portfolio_services

Use this tool to discover assets based on capabilities and use cases.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>asset_type</code></p></td><td><p>Asset type (string).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>max_results</code></p></td><td><p>Max results (integer).</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>query</code></p></td><td><p>Query (string).</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
