```json
      "percentile": "P95",
      "averageLatencyMs": 145,
      "latencyByApp": [
        {
          "appName": "customer-integration-app",
          "p95LatencyMs": 89
        }
      ]
    },
    "errors": {
      "totalErrors": 1250,
      "errorRate": 0.14,
      "errorsByType": [
        {
          "errorType": "Timeout",
          "count": 450
        },
        {
          "errorType": "Connection",
          "count": 320
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

## get_reuse_metrics

Get visibility into assets reuse across your organization. Drive efficiency, consistency, and cost
savings by reducing duplication. Includes sandbox and production environments. Metrics are a
year-to-date snapshot.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>includeReuseMetrics</code></p></td><td><p>Whether the output includes reuse metrics for applications. Default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "server_name": "https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mulesoft/mcp-server",
  "tool_name": "get_reuse_metrics",
  "arguments": {
    "includeReuseMetrics": true
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "reportingPeriod": "2024 Year-to-Date",
  "summary": {
    "totalAssets": 156,
    "reusedAssets": 89,
    "reuseRate": "57%",
    "costSavingsEstimate": "$45,000"
  },
  "reuseMetrics": {
    "byAssetType": [
      {
        "assetType": "mule-application",
        "totalAssets": 45,
        "reusedAssets": 28,
        "reuseRate": "62%",
        "avgReusesPerAsset": 3.2
      },
      {
        "assetType": "raml",
        "totalAssets": 32,
        "reusedAssets": 25,
        "reuseRate": "78%",
        "avgReusesPerAsset": 4.1
      },
      {
        "assetType": "connector",
        "totalAssets": 18,
        "reusedAssets": 15,
        "reuseRate": "83%",
        "avgReusesPerAsset": 5.7
      }
    ],
    "byBusinessGroup": [
      {
        "businessGroup": "Sales",
        "assetsCreated": 34,
        "assetsReused": 67,
        "reuseRatio": 1.97
      },
      {
        "businessGroup": "Marketing",
        "assetsCreated": 28,
        "assetsReused": 45,
        "reuseRatio": 1.61
      }
    ],
    "topReusedAssets": [
      {
        "assetName": "Customer API",
        "assetType": "raml",
        "reuseCount": 12,
        "lastReused": "2024-01-28"
      },
      {
        "assetName": "HTTP Basic Auth Policy",
        "assetType": "policy",
        "reuseCount": 8,
        "lastReused": "2024-01-30"
      }
    ]
  },
  "environments": ["Production", "Staging", "Development"],
  "recommendations": [
    "Consider promoting 'Payment Processing Flow' template to Exchange for wider reuse",
    "Customer API shows high reuse - consider creating additional variants",
    "15 assets have not been reused - review for consolidation opportunities"
  ]
}
```

## get_topics_agent_actions_metadata_instructions

Get metadata and instructions for enabling topics and agent actions from an OpenAI spec. Topics are
bundles of actions and sets of instructions agents use to complete tasks. Actions are tasks that the
agent can do, and instructions help the agent understand how to use actions.

Available only in Anypoint Code Builder.

This tool has no parameters.

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "get_topics_agent_actions_metadata_instructions",
  "arguments": {}
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "topics": [
    {
      "id": "api-management",
      "name": "API Management",
      "description": "Topics related to API management operations",
      "actions": [
        {
          "id": "create-api",
          "name": "Create API",
          "description": "Create a new API specification",
          "instructions": "Use this action to create a new API specification. Provide the API name, version, and specification format."
        }
      ],
      "instructions": "Use these actions to manage APIs in your organization."
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "lastUpdated": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  }
}
```

## implement_api_spec

Use APIkit flows to implement an API specification in a new integration project. You can implement a
specification from a local project or an Anypoint Exchange asset.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>mode</code></p></td><td><p>Determines the operation type. To implement API specs in a local workspace, use <code>local_scaffolding</code> mode. For APIs published to Anypoint Exchange, use <code>exchange_api_scaffolding</code>.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>projectName</code></p></td><td><p>The name for the integration project. Also used as the project folder name.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>projectPath</code></p></td><td><p>[<code>local_scaffolding</code> mode] The absolute path to the local API project to scaffold. The API project must contain a valid exchange.json and classifier.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>projectLocation</code></p></td><td><p>The path where the project folder is located. The default is the current workspace.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>groupId</code></p></td><td><p>[<code>exchange_api_scaffolding</code> mode] The group ID of the API specification in Anypoint Exchange.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>assetId</code></p></td><td><p>[<code>exchange_api_scaffolding</code> mode] The asset ID of the API specification in Anypoint Exchange.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>version</code></p></td><td><p>[<code>exchange_api_scaffolding</code> mode] The version of the API specification, for example '1.4.0'.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>classifier</code></p></td><td><p>[<code>exchange_api_scaffolding</code> mode] The classifier of the API specification. Fetched from Anypoint Exchange if not provided.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>type</code></p></td><td><p>[<code>exchange_api_scaffolding</code> mode] The type or packaging of the API specification. Fetched from Anypoint Exchange if not provided.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>main</code></p></td><td><p>[<code>exchange_api_scaffolding</code> mode] The main file of the API specification. Fetched from Anypoint Exchange if not provided.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>runtimeVersion</code></p></td><td><p>The runtime version to use. Uses the workspace configuration or the latest version available if not specified.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>jdkVersion</code></p></td><td><p>The JDK version to use. Uses the workspace configuration or the latest available if not specified.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>mavenGroupId</code></p></td><td><p>The Maven group ID for the project. Defaults to the group ID of the API specification or workspace setting.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "server_name": "https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mulesoft/mcp-server",
  "tool_name": "implement_api_spec",
  "arguments": {
    "groupId": "com.acme.apis",
    "assetId": "customer-management-api",
    "version": "1.2.0",
    "projectName": "customer-management-implementation",
    "projectLocation": "/Users/developer/mule-projects",
    "classifier": "raml",
    "type": "zip",
    "main": "api.raml",
    "runtimeVersion": "4.6.0",
    "jdkVersion": "17",
    "mavenGroupId": "com.acme.implementations"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Mule project successfully scaffolded from API specification",
  "details": {
    "projectPath": "/Users/developer/mule-projects/customer-management-implementation",
    "apiSpecification": {
      "groupId": "com.acme.apis",
      "assetId": "customer-management-api",
      "version": "1.2.0",
      "classifier": "raml",
      "mainFile": "api.raml"
    },
    "projectConfiguration": {
      "name": "customer-management-implementation",
      "runtimeVersion": "4.6.0",
      "jdkVersion": "17",
      "mavenGroupId": "com.acme.implementations",
      "artifactId": "customer-management-implementation"
    },
    "generatedFiles": [
      "pom.xml",
      "mule-artifact.json",
      "src/main/mule/implementation.xml",
      "src/main/resources/api/api.raml",
      "src/main/resources/application.yaml",
      "src/test/munit/implementation-test-suite.xml"
    ],
    "endpoints": [
      {
        "method": "GET",
        "path": "/customers",
        "description": "Get all customers"
      },
      {
        "method": "POST",
        "path": "/customers",
        "description": "Create a new customer"
      },
      {
        "method": "GET",
        "path": "/customers/{customerId}",
        "description": "Get customer by ID"
      },
      {
        "method": "PUT",
        "path": "/customers/{customerId}",
        "description": "Update customer"
      },
      {
        "method": "DELETE",
        "path": "/customers/{customerId}",
        "description": "Delete customer"
      }
    ],
    "nextSteps": [
      "Open the project in your workspace using the open_project tool",
      "Implement business logic for each endpoint",
      "Configure database connections if needed",
      "Add error handling and validation",
      "Test the implementation using MUnit"
    ]
  }
}
```

## list_api_instances

Get information for instances in an environment. You can also filter by an instance ID or label and
include applied or available policies. Use the `page` parameter to paginate results.

Prompt tip: To get information for all instances, revise results using the `page` parameter.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organizationId</code></p></td><td><p>ID of the organization where instances reside. Default is the ID of the default organization.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environmentName</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the environment where instances reside. Default is the name of the default environment for the organization specified in <code>organizationId</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>apiInstanceId</code></p></td><td><p>Instance ID of the API.</p></td><td><p>No</p><p>Required if <code>includeAvailablePolicies=true</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>apiInstanceLabel</code></p></td><td><p>Label or name of the API instance.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>page</code></p></td><td><p>Page number of the results. Each page contains information for up to 10 instances.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>includeAppliedPolicies</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to include applied policies in results.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>includeAvailablePolicies</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to include available policies in results. Requires <code>apiInstanceId</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request (list all instances)

```json
{
  "server_name": "https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mulesoft/mcp-server",
  "tool_name": "list_api_instances",
  "arguments": {
    "environmentName": "Production",
    "page": 1,
    "includeAppliedPolicies": true
  }
}
```

Example request (specific instance with available policies)

```json
{
  "server_name": "https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mulesoft/mcp-server",
  "tool_name": "list_api_instances",
  "arguments": {
    "environmentName": "Production",
    "apiInstanceId": "12345",
    "page": 1,
    "includeAppliedPolicies": true,
    "includeAvailablePolicies": true
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "environmentName": "Production",
  "page": 1,
  "totalPages": 3,
  "totalInstances": 25,
  "instances": [
    {
      "apiInstanceId": "12345",
      "apiInstanceLabel": "Customer Management API",
      "assetId": "customer-api",
      "assetVersion": "1.0.0",
      "groupId": "com.mycompany.apis",
      "runtimeEngine": "mule4",
      "endpointUri": "https://backend.example.com/api/customers",
      "consumerEndpoint": "https://api.example.com/customers",
      "status": "active",
      "appliedPolicies": [
        {
          "policyId": "rate-limiting-001",
          "policyName": "Rate Limiting",
          "version": "1.2.0",
          "status": "applied"
        },
        {
          "policyId": "basic-auth-002",
          "policyName": "HTTP Basic Authentication",
          "version": "1.0.0",
          "status": "applied"
        }
      ],
      "availablePolicies": [
        {
          "policyName": "OAuth 2.0 Access Token Enforcement",
          "assetId": "oauth-2-access-token-enforcement",
          "groupId": "68ef9520-24e9-4cf2-b2f5-620025690913",
          "version": "3.2.2",
          "description": "Validates OAuth 2.0 access tokens"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "apiInstanceId": "67890",
      "apiInstanceLabel": "Product Catalog API",
      "assetId": "product-api",
      "assetVersion": "2.1.0",
      "groupId": "com.mycompany.apis",
      "runtimeEngine": "mule4",
      "endpointUri": "https://backend.example.com/api/products",
      "consumerEndpoint": "https://api.example.com/products",
      "status": "active",
      "appliedPolicies": []
    }
  ]
}
```

## uc_add_pagination

Add pagination support to a specific CCM operation. Takes the operation file path and pagination
configuration parameters and creates a paginated operation module.

Available only in Connector Builder.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>operationFilePath</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path (URI) to an operation file inside the CCM operations directory.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>type</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Pagination strategy to apply. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>offset</code></p></li><li><p><code>cursor</code></p></li><li><p><code>page</code></p></li><li><p><code>timestamp</code></p></li><li><p><code>custom</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>itemsField</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the array of items in the response, for example, <code>data.items</code> or <code>results</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>offsetParam</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the offset parameter, for example, <code>offset</code> or <code>skip</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='offset'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>initialOffsetValue</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Starting offset value (typically <code>0</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='offset'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>pageNumberParam</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the page number parameter, for example, <code>page</code> or <code>pageNumber</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='page'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>initialPageNumber</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Starting page number (typically <code>0</code> or <code>1</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='page'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>timestampParam</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the timestamp parameter, for example, <code>since</code> or <code>after</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='timestamp'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>cursorParam</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the cursor parameter, for example, <code>cursor</code> or <code>nextToken</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='cursor'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>referenceType</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Type of reference used for cursor pagination. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>marker</code> for a simple marker or token value</p></li><li><p><code>uri</code> for a URI containing the next page reference</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='cursor'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>referenceExpression</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path expression to extract the next cursor value from the response, for example, <code>pagination.nextCursor</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='cursor'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>projectRootPath</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Absolute path to the connector project directory. Defaults to the workspace root folder.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "uc_add_pagination",
  "arguments": {
    "operationFilePath": "/Users/you/workspace/stripe-connector/operations/list-customers.yaml",
    "type": "cursor",
    "cursorParam": "starting_after",
    "referenceType": "marker",
    "referenceExpression": "data[-1].id",
    "itemsField": "data"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Pagination added to operation."
}
```

## uc_connector_lifecycle

Manage connector lifecycle operations. Use `type='build'` to build the connector project using the
extensionâs bundled Maven (`mvn install`). Use `type='publish'` to publish the connector to Anypoint
Exchange (`mvn deploy`). For publish, the tool automatically determines execution mode: granular
mode if all required configuration is available (executes publish directly), or workflow mode if
`organizationId` or `repositoryId` is missing (returns a workflow plan to collect configuration).

Available only in Connector Builder.

> [!NOTE] This tool was previously named `uc_publish_connector`. The renamed tool also supports the
> `build` action.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>type</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Lifecycle action to perform. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>build</code> to compile and package the connector project</p></li><li><p><code>publish</code> to deploy the connector to Anypoint Exchange</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>cleanBeforeBuild</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to run <code>clean</code> before <code>install</code>. Used when <code>type='build'</code>. Defaults to the corresponding VS Code setting.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>workflowCustomization</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Specific workflow customizations, preferences, or requirements for the publish process. Used when <code>type='publish'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organizationId</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Anypoint Exchange organization ID. If not provided, the tool checks the POM file. If missing there too, workflow mode is triggered. Used when <code>type='publish'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>repositoryId</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Private Exchange repository ID. Same fallback behavior as <code>organizationId</code>. Used when <code>type='publish'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>profiles</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Maven profiles to activate for deployment. Defaults to <code>['private-exchange-deploy']</code>. Used when <code>type='publish'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>projectRootPath</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Absolute path to the connector project directory. Defaults to the workspace root folder.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "uc_connector_lifecycle",
  "arguments": {
    "type": "publish",
    "organizationId": "abc123",
    "repositoryId": "xyz789"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Connector published to Anypoint Exchange."
}
```

## uc_create_connector_workflow

Create a complete LinkWeave connector end-to-end with guided workflow planning. Supports two flows:
generating an API spec from documentation, or using an existing API spec file. Returns a detailed
workflow plan with numbered steps to execute sequentially.

Available only in Connector Builder.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>workflowCustomization</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any specific workflow customizations, preferences, or requirements for the connector-creation process.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>flow</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Flow selection. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>generate-spec</code> to generate an API spec from documentation</p></li><li><p><code>existing-spec</code> if the user has an existing API spec file</p></li><li><p><code>auto-detect</code> to determine automatically</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>existingApiSpec</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Absolute path to existing API spec file (OpenAPI/Swagger YAML or JSON) if using <code>existing-spec</code> flow.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>mode</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Execution mode. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>standard</code> (default) runs interactively with user confirmation steps.</p></li><li><p><code>connectorless</code> runs autonomously and only pauses to collect credentials.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>platform</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the SaaS platform, for example, <code>Stripe</code> or <code>ServiceNow</code>. Pre-populates the workflow when provided.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>useCases</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>List of integration use cases, for example, <code>Sync customer data</code>. Pre-populates the workflow when provided.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>documentation</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>List of API documentation URLs. Pre-populates the workflow when provided.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request (generate spec flow)

```json
{
  "name": "uc_create_connector_workflow",
  "arguments": {
    "flow": "generate-spec",
    "workflowCustomization": "I want to create a connector for Salesforce CRM with focus on lead and opportunity management. Include authentication setup and error handling best practices."
  }
}
```

Example request (existing spec flow)

```json
{
  "name": "uc_create_connector_workflow",
  "arguments": {
    "flow": "existing-spec",
    "existingApiSpec": "/Users/developer/apis/salesforce-leads-api.yaml",
    "workflowCustomization": "Use this Salesforce API spec to generate a connector with enhanced lead management capabilities."
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "flow": "generate-spec",
  "workflowPlan": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "title": "Gather API Documentation",
      "description": "Collect official API documentation URLs and identify key use cases",
      "tool": "uc_manage_api_spec",
      "estimatedTime": "5-10 minutes"
    },
    {
      "step": 2,
      "title": "Generate API Specification",
      "description": "Create OpenAPI 3.0 specification from documentation and use cases",
      "tool": "uc_manage_api_spec",
      "estimatedTime": "10-15 minutes"
    },
    {
      "step": 3,
      "title": "Generate Connector Project",
      "description": "Create LinkWeave connector project with CCM and Anypoint phases",
      "tool": "uc_generate_connector",
      "estimatedTime": "15-20 minutes"
    },
    {
      "step": 4,
      "title": "Test and Validate",
      "description": "Test connector functionality and validate against use cases",
      "tool": "test_connection",
      "estimatedTime": "10-15 minutes"
    }
  ],
  "totalEstimatedTime": "40-60 minutes",
  "nextSteps": "Begin with step 1 by providing the platform name, use cases, and documentation URLs to the uc_manage_api_spec tool.",
  "customizations": [
    "Focus on lead and opportunity management endpoints",
    "Include OAuth 2.0 authentication configuration",
    "Implement comprehensive error handling",
    "Add retry logic for rate limiting"
  ]
}
```

## uc_generate_connector

Generate a complete LinkWeave connector from an API spec. Supports flexible multi-phase generation
including scaffolding, CCM (Canonical Connectivity Model), Anypoint Connector, and Flow Connector.
All endpoints from the API spec are used. Can execute phases individually or in combination.

Available only in Connector Builder.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>apiSpec</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Absolute path to the API spec file (OpenAPI/Swagger YAML or JSON).</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p><p>Required for <code>ccm</code> phase.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>projectName</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name for the connector project.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p><p>Required for <code>ccm</code> phase.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Description for the connector project.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p><p>Required for <code>ccm</code> phase.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>phases</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of phases to execute - any combination of <code>ccm</code>, <code>anypoint</code>, and <code>flow</code>. Phases execute in order. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ccm</code> for Canonical Connectivity Model</p></li><li><p><code>anypoint</code> for Anypoint Connector module</p></li><li><p><code>flow</code> for Flow Connector module</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>projectRootPath</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Absolute path to the directory where the connector project should be created (for the <code>ccm</code> phase) or found (for the <code>anypoint</code> or <code>flow</code> phases). Defaults to the workspace root folder.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "uc_generate_connector",
  "arguments": {
    "apiSpec": "/Users/developer/apis/salesforce-api.yaml",
    "projectName": "Salesforce Integration Connector",
    "description": "Custom connector for Salesforce CRM integration with enhanced features.",
    "phases": ["ccm", "anypoint", "flow"]
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Connector generated successfully.",
  "projectPath": "/Users/you/workspace/stripe-connector",
  "phasesExecuted": ["ccm", "anypoint", "flow"]
}
```

## uc_generate_tests

Generate tests for connector modules. Each requested module is configured to use either a mock
connection (no auth) or a real connection (requires a `profileId`). At least one module must be
included. Always call `uc_get_test_generation_info` first to determine which modules are available
and whether tests already exist.

Available only in Connector Builder.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>modulesConfig</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of modules to test. Skipped modules are omitted from this array. At least one module is required.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>modulesConfig[].moduleType</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Module type to test. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>CCM</code> for the connectivity model</p></li><li><p><code>ACM</code> for the Anypoint Connector module</p></li><li><p><code>FCM</code> for the Flow Connector module</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>modulesConfig[].useMock</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a mock connection. Set to <code>true</code> for a mock connection (no auth required) or <code>false</code> for a real backend connection (requires <code>profileId</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>profileId</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Connection profile ID returned by <code>uc_list_connection_profiles</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when any module has <code>useMock=false</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>forceOverwrite</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to overwrite existing test files. Set to <code>true</code> only after the user explicitly confirms overwriting. Defaults to <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when existing tests are present.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "uc_generate_tests",
  "arguments": {
    "modulesConfig": [
      { "moduleType": "CCM", "useMock": true },
      { "moduleType": "ACM", "useMock": false }
    ],
    "profileId": "dev-stripe",
    "forceOverwrite": false
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Tests generated for CCM and ACM modules."
}
```

## uc_get_test_generation_info

Get information before generating tests. Returns the list of available connector modules (CCM, ACM,
FCM) and reports whether tests already exist for each. Always call this tool before
`uc_generate_tests` so you can determine which modules to test and whether existing test files would
be overwritten.

Available only in Connector Builder.

This tool takes no parameters.

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "uc_get_test_generation_info",
  "arguments": {}
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "availableModules": ["CCM", "ACM", "FCM"],
  "testsExist": false
}
```

## uc_list_connection_profiles

List all available connection profiles with their IDs, names, authentication types, and
descriptions. Use this tool to discover which profiles are available for executing operations or
generating tests against a real backend. The profile ID returned by this tool is required for the
`execute` type in `uc_manage_operations` and for real-connection test generation in
`uc_generate_tests`.

Available only in Connector Builder.

This tool takes no parameters.

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "uc_list_connection_profiles",
  "arguments": {}
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "profiles": [
    {
      "id": "dev-stripe",
      "name": "Stripe Developer",
      "authenticationType": "BEARER",
      "description": "Stripe API developer credentials"
    }
  ]
}
```

## uc_manage_api_spec

Manage API spec operations. Use `type='generate'` (default) to perform API research and produce both
an `api-reference.md` artifact and a complete OpenAPI 3.0 YAML spec. Use `type='research'` to
perform API research only, producing a comprehensive `api-reference.md` with entity dependency
graphs, CRUD endpoint catalogs, and field-level detail. Use `type='validate'` to validate an
existing OpenAPI spec against a live API by executing real HTTP requests, comparing responses
against expected schemas, auto-fixing mismatches, and generating a validation report. If no
parameters are used, the agent prompts for input.

Available only in Connector Builder.

> [!NOTE] This tool was previously named `uc_generate_api_spec`. The renamed tool also supports the
> `research` and `validate` modes.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>type</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Type of operation. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>generate</code> (default): Performs API research and generates an OpenAPI YAML spec.</p></li><li><p><code>research</code>: Performs API research only and produces an <code>api-reference.md</code> artifact.</p></li><li><p><code>validate</code>: Validates an OpenAPI spec against a live API and generates a validation report.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>platform</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the SaaS platform or service, for example, <code>Salesforce</code>, <code>ServiceNow</code>, <code>PayPal</code>, or <code>Stripe</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>useCases</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>List of integration use cases, ordered by priority. Each use case describes a specific integration scenario, for example, <code>Sync customer data</code> or <code>Process invoices</code>. If specified, a minimum of one is required.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>documentation</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>List of official API documentation URLs. Must be valid URLs starting with <code>http://</code> or <code>https://</code>, for example, <code>https://developers.stripe.com/api</code>. If specified, a minimum of one is required.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>additionalRequirements</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any additional requirements, constraints, or preferences for the API spec, for example, specific authentication methods, rate limiting considerations, and version preferences.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>mode</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Execution mode. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>standard</code> (default): Runs interactively.</p></li><li><p><code>connectorless</code>: Runs autonomously with minimal user interaction.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>specPath</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the OpenAPI YAML spec file to validate. Used when <code>type='validate'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>apiReferencePath</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the <code>api-reference.md</code> file containing entity details, dependency graphs, and request/response schemas. Used when <code>type='validate'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>baseUrl</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>API base URL override. If not provided, extracted from the OpenAPI spec or <code>api-reference.md</code>. Used when <code>type='validate'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>authHeader</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Authorization header value, for example, <code>Bearer &lt;token&gt;</code>. If not provided, the tool prompts for credentials. Used when <code>type='validate'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>operationFilter</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>List of specific operation IDs to validate. If empty, validates all operations. Used when <code>type='validate'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "uc_manage_api_spec",
  "arguments": {
    "type": "generate",
    "platform": "Stripe",
    "useCases": ["Sync customers into our CRM", "Create a charge and issue a refund"],
    "documentation": [
      "https://docs.stripe.com/api",
      "https://docs.stripe.com/api/customers",
      "https://docs.stripe.com/api/charges",
      "https://docs.stripe.com/api/refunds"
    ],
    "additionalRequirements": "OpenAPI 3.0 YAML. Use bearer token auth (Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>). Include common error responses (400/401/403/404/409/429/500). Model pagination where applicable."
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Generated api-reference.md and OpenAPI 3.0 spec from documentation and use cases.",
  "platform": "Stripe",
  "apiReferencePath": "/Users/you/workspace/stripe-spec/api-reference.md",
  "openApiYamlPath": "/Users/you/workspace/stripe-spec/openapi.yaml"
}
```

## uc_manage_operations

Manage connector operations. Use `type='list'` to enumerate available operations, `type='get'` to
retrieve one operationâs input and output schemas, or `type='execute'` to run an operation against a
connection profile. To execute an operation, first call `uc_list_connection_profiles` to retrieve a
profile ID.

Available only in Connector Builder.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>type</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Action to perform. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>list</code> to list all operations</p></li><li><p><code>get</code> to get details for one operation</p></li><li><p><code>execute</code> to run an operation with a connection profile</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>operationName</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Operation name or ID.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='get'</code> or <code>type='execute'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>profileId</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Connection profile ID returned by <code>uc_list_connection_profiles</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when <code>type='execute'</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>input</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Operation input parameters as a JSON object. Use <code>type='get'</code> first to discover the expected schema.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "uc_manage_operations",
  "arguments": {
    "type": "execute",
    "operationName": "getCustomer",
    "profileId": "dev-stripe",
    "input": { "id": "cus_123" }
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "response": {
    "id": "cus_123",
    "name": "Acme Corp",
    "email": "billing@acme.example"
  }
}
```

## update_mule_application

Update a Mule application deployed to CloudHub 2.0 or Runtime Fabric. Update runtime version,
configuration, and other application settings.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>applicationId</code></p></td><td><p>The ID of the application to update with the latest Mule runtime patch release.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environmentId</code></p></td><td><p>The ID of the environment where the application is deployed.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>organizationId</code></p></td><td><p>The ID of the organization where the application is deployed.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>runtimeVersion</code></p></td><td><p>Mule runtime version to use. For example, <code>4.9.4:2e-java17</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>hasPersistentObjectStore</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to enable persistent object store. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>replicaSize</code></p></td><td><p>Replica size for CloudHub 2.0 applications. Express size in vCores or instance type size.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>cpuReserved</code></p></td><td><p>Reserved vCPUs for the application. For example, 1.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>cpuLimit</code></p></td><td><p>Maximum vCPUs for the application. For example, 2.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>memory</code></p></td><td><p>Memory allocation for the allocation in GB. For example, 1.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>replicas</code></p></td><td><p>Number of replicas.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>minReplicas</code></p></td><td><p>If <code>hasHighAvailability=true</code> or <code>hasPerformanceOptimization=true</code>, this value is the minimum number of replicas.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>maxReplicas</code></p></td><td><p>If <code>hasHighAvailability=true</code> or <code>hasPerformanceOptimization=true</code>, this value is the maximum number of replicas.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>hasAutoscaling</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to enable autoscaling. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>hasTracing</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to enable application tracing. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>enforceDeployingReplicasAcrossNodes</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to enforce deploying replicas across nodes (Runtime Fabric only). The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>deploymentModel</code></p></td><td><p>The deployment strategy for application updates. Use <code>rolling</code> for zero-downtime rolling updates or <code>recreate</code> to stop and restart all instances.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>hasClustering</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to enable clustering for the application. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>stopApplication</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to stop the application. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>startApplication</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to start the application. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>hasHighAvailability</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to enable high availability for the application. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>hasPerformanceOptimization</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to enable performance optimization. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>isSecure</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to enable advanced security features. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "update_mule_application",
  "arguments": {
    "applicationId": "3f2d8b1a-0f2e-4f6c-9e44-6f3b2c1a9c10",
    "environmentId": "b7c9f6f2-1f8d-4c2e-a7b0-3d6c9a1b2e34",
    "runtimeVersion": "4.9.4:2e-java17",
    "replicaSize": "0.5",
    "replicas": 2,
    "hasAutoscaling": true,
    "minReplicas": 2,
    "maxReplicas": 4,
    "hasTracing": true,
    "deploymentModel": "rolling"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Application update initiated.",
  "applicationId": "3f2d8b1a-0f2e-4f6c-9e44-6f3b2c1a9c10",
  "environmentId": "b7c9f6f2-1f8d-4c2e-a7b0-3d6c9a1b2e34",
  "deploymentModel": "rolling",
  "status": "updating"
}
```

## validate_api_against_ruleset

Validate an API specification in your project against governance rulesets or rules that you specify.
If you specify all rulesets, the tool uses an empty `rules` parameter to validate all rulesets.
Similarly, if you specify no ruleset, the tool uses an empty `rules` parameter. To find rules or
rulesets that you specify, this tool calls the `list_governance_rulesets` tool.

Available only in Anypoint Code Builder.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>projectPath</code></p></td><td><p>Path where the project folder is located. The default is the current workspace. For absolute paths, use <code>file:</code> followed by three slash characters.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>rules</code></p></td><td><p>Array of API rules to validate. If empty, the tool validates all rulesets in <code>projectPath</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>syncCentralizedRulesets</code></p></td><td><p>Whether the tool syncs centralized rulesets before validating the API specification. The default value is true.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request (validate all rulesets)

```json
{
  "name": "validate_api_against_ruleset",
  "arguments": {
    "projectPath": "/Users/developer/projects/pet-store-api",
    "rules": [],
    "syncCentralizedRulesets": true
  }
}
```

Example request (validate specific rules)

```json
{
  "name": "validate_api_against_ruleset",
  "arguments": {
    "projectPath": "/Users/developer/projects/pet-store-api",
    "rules": [
      {
        "name": "api-title-required",
        "rulePath": "/Users/developer/.mule/rulesets/api-governance-ruleset/rules/api-title-required.js"
      },
      {
        "name": "api-version-required",
        "rulePath": "/Users/developer/.mule/rulesets/api-governance-ruleset/rules/api-version-required.js"
      }
    ],
    "syncCentralizedRulesets": true
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "validationResults": {
    "totalRules": 2,
    "passed": 1,
    "failed": 1,
    "warnings": 0
  },
  "results": [
    {
      "ruleName": "api-title-required",
      "status": "passed",
      "message": "API title is present"
    },
    {
      "ruleName": "api-version-required",
      "status": "failed",
      "message": "API version is missing",
      "severity": "error",
      "location": {
        "file": "pet-store-api.yaml",
        "line": 2,
        "column": 5
      }
    }
  ],
  "summary": "Validation completed with 1 error"
}
```

## validate_project

Validate the current project. For example, an API spec or an agent network project. This tool
doesnât validate against API Governance rulesets. To validate against those rulesets, use the
`validate_api_against_ruleset` tool.

Available only in Anypoint Code Builder.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>projectPath</code></p></td><td><p>Path where the project folder is located. The default is the current workspace. For absolute paths, use <code>file:</code> followed by three slash characters, for example, <code>file:///Users/myUser/workspace/</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Example request

```json
{
  "name": "validate_project",
  "arguments": {
    "projectPath": "/Users/developer/projects/pet-store-api"
  }
}
```

Example response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "projectType": "api-spec",
  "validationResults": {
    "syntaxValid": true,
    "structureValid": true,
    "errors": [],
    "warnings": [
      {
        "message": "Missing description for endpoint /pets",
        "location": {
          "file": "pet-store-api.yaml",
          "line": 15
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "message": "Project validation completed successfully"
}
```

## See Also

- [Using MuleSoft DX MCP Server](using-mulesoft-mcp-server)
---

# Using MuleSoft DX MCP Server

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft DX MCP Server integrates with MuleSoft Vibes so that you can build, deploy, and manage
projects using natural language prompts. Whether you use Anypoint Code Builder, Cursor, Windsurf, or
another MCP-compatible IDE, you keep the full Mule application lifecycle in your editor.

To learn more about MuleSoft Vibes, see
[Get Started with MuleSoft Vibes](../anypoint-code-builder/vibes-get-started).

To learn more about the MuleSoft DX MCP Server tools, see
[MuleSoft DX MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools).

## Before You Begin

Before you get started, make sure you meet these prerequisites.

- If youâre using Anypoint Code Builder, make sure that you:
  - [Set up and access the desktop IDE](../anypoint-code-builder/start-acb).
  - Set the
    [required Anypoint Code Builder permissions](../anypoint-code-builder/start-configure-permissions#permissions),
    including the Mule Developer Generative AI User permission.
  - [Enable Agentforce in Access Management](../access-management/enabling-agentforce).

- If youâre using Cursor, Windsurf, or any MCP-compatible IDE, make sure you set up and configure
  MuleSoft DX MCP Server for your IDE, including all permissions. See
  [Getting Started with MuleSoft DX MCP Server](getting-started).

## Example: Create a Network Using MuleSoft Vibes

1.  In the Anypoint Code Builder activity bar (or similar location in your IDE), click the agent
    icon ![](_images/af-acb-dev-agent-icon.png).
2.  In the prompt field, describe your agent network, including the brokers, agents, MCP servers,
    and LLMs you want to connect. MuleSoft Vibes does the rest.

For example, consider this prompt entered using the agentâs
[Plan mode](../anypoint-code-builder/vibes-get-started#plan-and-act-modes):
`Create a new agent network project called "Employee Onboarding." Add HR agent and CRM agent.`

The agent asks for `projectPath`, which is a required parameter for the
[`create_agent_network_project`](reference-mcp-tools#create-agent-network-project) tool.

After successfully running, the agent creates an `agent-network.yaml` file and responds with a final
success summary.

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "projectPath": "/Users/test/src/mulesoft/mulesoft-mcp-server/Employee Onboarding",
  "message": "Employee Onboarding agent network created successfully",
  "components": {
    "agents": ["hr-agent", "crm-agent"],
    "brokers": ["onboarding-coordinator"],
    "connections": 1,
    "filesCreated": ["agent-network.yaml", "exchange.json", "README.md"]
  },
  "nextSteps": [
    "Deploy to runtime environment using deploy_agent_network",
    "Publish to Exchange using publish_agent_network_assets",
    "Open project in workspace using open_project"
  ],
  "validationStatus": "passed"
}
```

## Example Prompts

Use these example prompts to guide MuleSoft Vibes in completing common tasks. Customize the values
to match your project or environment.

### Application Deployment and Projects

- **Task**: Deploy an app to CloudHub or Runtime Fabric.

  **Prompt**: Deploy the \[application_name\] app from Anypoint Exchange.

- **Task**: Create an integration project.

  **Prompt**: Create a Mule project that creates an order in NetSuite every time an opportunity in
  Salesforce is updated to the âClosed Wonâ stage.

- **Task**: Deploy an integration application.

  **Prompt**: Deploy \[application_name\] app in the current project. Deploy it with high security,
  high availability, and performance optimized settings.

- **Task**: Modify an existing application.

  **Prompt**: Update \[application_name\] so that it meets \[specific business requirements\].

- **Task**: Generate an MCP server using Anypoint connectors.

  **Prompt**: Generate an MCP server that exposes three tools from the following operations:
  Workday - Absence Management - Get Time Off Plan Balances, Workday - Absence Management - Enter
  Time Off, and Google Calendar - Create Calendar Event.

### Exchange Asset Management

- **Task**: Create an asset in Exchange.

  **Prompt**: Publish \[this asset type\] in Exchange using this \[name\].

- **Task**: Search for an asset in Exchange.

  **Prompt**: Do I have any assets for order notifications in Exchange?

- **Task**: Search for an asset within a specific organization.

  **Prompt**: Find \[asset name\] in \[organization name\].

### API Instances

- **Task**: Create an app instance and apply a policy.

  **Prompt**: Create an instance for the \[application_name\] and apply the rate limiting policy to
  that instance.

- **Task**: View performance metrics for your APIs or apps.

  **Prompt**: Which apps generated the most errors this month?

### Custom Metadata

- **Task**: Set custom metadata that uses sample data.

  **Prompt**: Can you help me define custom metadata on the following component? This is how the
  sample data looks: \[sample data\].

- **Task**: Set custom metadata using a file.

  **Prompt**: Define custom metadata using the sample file below and attach it to \[processor_name\]
  @\[path_to_file\].

### Agent Network Operations

- **Task**: Create an Agent Network

  **Prompt**: Create an Agent Network for called "Employee Onboarding".

- **Task**: Publish an Agent Network.

  **Prompt**: Publish the "Employee Onboarding" Agent Network project to Exchange.

- **Task**: Deploy an instance of the "Employee Onboarding" Agent Network to staging.

  **Prompt**: Deploy this Agent Network project.
---

# Using MuleSoft Platform MCP Server

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Get governed access to your entire agentic landscape, including APIs, agents, MCP servers, and LLMs,
to accelerate production-ready AI at scale using MuleSoft Platform MCP Server. Use it to you scan,
discover, govern, and monitor services across the infrastructure you already run. Integrations with
MuleSoft Vibes or various AI clients, including Claude, Windsurf, and Cursor enables you to access
your assets wherever you want.

To learn more about how to set up MuleSoft Platform MCP Server, see
[Getting Started with MuleSoft Platform MCP Server](getting-started-platform).

To learn more about MuleSoft Platform MCP Server tools, see
[MuleSoft Platform MCP Server Tool Reference](reference-mcp-tools-platform).

## Before You Begin

Before you get started, make sure you complete the setup and configuration steps, including
permissions, in [Getting Started with MuleSoft Platform MCP Server](getting-started-platform).

## Example: Applying Policies Across MuleSoft Platform

1.  From your IDE or AI assistant, authenticate into your org.
2.  In the prompt field, ask the agent to apply a policy to a specific API.

For example, use this prompt: `Help me set up a rate-limiting policy on my Stripe API.`

The agent calls `prepare_policy_creation` to fetch available policies for the target API instance:

```json
{
  "name": "prepare_policy_creation",
  "arguments": {
    "organization_id": "<organization_id>",
    "asset_id": "<asset_id>",
    "policy_name_hint": "rate-limiting"
  }
}
```

This returns a list of applicable policies for the target API instance. The user selects a policy,
and the agent calls `get_policy_template_form` to present configuration options. After the user
specifies the policy configuration, the agent calls `apply_policy_to_instance` to apply the policy
immediately.

## Example Prompts

Use prompts like these to explore key platform workflows:

- Search and browse: `Show me all payment-related APIs across my organization.`
- Govern and secure:
  `Create a governance strategy that applies a PII detection policy to all customer-facing agents.`
- Monitor and analyze: `Which LLMs consumed the most requests last week?`
